 succeeded in 0ms:
     1	# FFI hardening — scenarios S6–S11
     2	
     3	See [`scenarios.md`](./scenarios.md) for S1–S5 and conventions.
     4	Numeric thresholds are in [`gates.md`](./gates.md).
     5	
     6	---
     7	
     8	## S6. Capability lifecycle storms — start/stop/restart
     9	
    10	**The bug shape.** `start` → `stop` → `start` cycles leave thread
    11	handles dangling; relay-worker generations get confused; the second
    12	`start` brings up a phantom relay worker.
    13	
    14	**Setup.** For each capability handle (today: the relay role pair
    15	Content + Indexer, plus the actor itself), run **1,000
    16	start/stop/restart cycles** in 5 minutes. Cycle:
    17	`nmp_app_start → wait 100ms → nmp_app_stop → wait 100ms → nmp_app_start → wait 100ms`.
    18	
    19	**Threading.** Caller drives cycles; actor receives Start/Stop;
    20	relay worker threads spawn and join repeatedly.
    21	
    22	**Assertions.**
    23	1. Thread count after 1,000 cycles ≤ baseline + 2 (actor + listener
    24	   only; relay workers gone after every Stop).
    25	2. **Idempotency (bible #7):** two consecutive Starts produce one
    26	   set of relay workers (verified by counting `RelayControl` entries).
    27	3. Generation counter `next_relay_generation` strictly monotonic, no
    28	   wrap (`u64` is fine for 1,000 cycles; harness verifies anyway).
    29	4. RSS growth ≤ **2 MB** over 1,000 cycles.
    30	5. No deadlocks (harness 5-s timeout per cycle).
    31	
    32	**Runner.** Rust harness only — these are pure FFI symbol calls.
    33	
    34	**Numeric gate.** See gates.md §G-S6.
    35	
    36	---
    37	
    38	## S7. Error-shape exhaustion — every typed FFI error path
    39	
    40	**The bug shape.** A typed FFI error path produces an uncaught
    41	exception, a crash, or — per the §7.2 finding in the parent doc —
    42	*silent loss*: invalid input is dropped without any state field
    43	surfacing the problem.
    44	
    45	**Setup.** For every `nmp_app_*` symbol that takes a `*const c_char`
    46	input, exercise the full set of invalid inputs:
    47	
    48	| Symbol | Invalid inputs to test |
    49	|---|---|
    50	| `nmp_app_open_author` | NULL, "", " ", "not-hex", 63-char hex, 65-char hex, UTF-8 with non-hex chars |
    51	| `nmp_app_open_thread` | same shapes |
    52	| `nmp_app_open_firehose_tag` | NULL, "" (others valid; tag is unconstrained) |
    53	| `nmp_app_claim_profile` | NULL/empty/non-hex pubkey × {NULL, "", "valid"} consumer_id |
    54	| `nmp_app_release_profile` | same matrix; also: release without prior claim |
    55	| `nmp_app_close_author` / `_thread` | same |
    56	| any `_app` arg | NULL |
    57	
    58	Note: freed-pointer (use-after-free) probes are **not** included.
    59	Calling any `nmp_app_*` with a dangling pointer after `nmp_app_free`
    60	is undefined behavior on raw FFI — the harness cannot make a
    61	no-crash assertion for UB. A handle registry to convert UB into a
    62	recoverable error is deferred to M14 (UniFFI migration); tracked as
    63	an open question in `ffi-hardening.md` §10.
    64	
    65	**Threading.** Caller. Pure FFI exercise.
    66	
    67	**Assertions.**
    68	1. Zero crashes / SIGSEGV / SIGABRT across the full matrix.
    69	2. NULL app pointer calls: **silent no-op only** (early return in
    70	   `app_ref()`). No toast assertion for NULL app — there is no state
    71	   channel to deliver a toast when the app pointer is null.
    72	3. Every other silent-no-op validation failure (invalid pubkey,
    73	   empty string, non-hex input) produces a **toast field** in the
    74	   next emit (post §7.2 toast-bridge addition).
    75	4. Every typed error path's toast string is non-empty and
    76	   actionable (regex match against the catalog in
    77	   `docs/perf/m10.5/error-catalog.md`, generated by this scenario).
    78	5. No error path leaks heap memory (Instruments-Allocations delta = 0
    79	   across the matrix).
    80	
    81	**Runner.** Rust harness primary, XCUITest secondary (UI assertion
    82	on toast banner rendering).
    83	
    84	**Numeric gate.** See gates.md §G-S7.
    85	
    86	---
    87	
    88	## S8. Subscription planner DOS
    89	
    90	**The bug shape.** A pathological app opens 10,000 distinct views
    91	in 1 s; the planner compiles 10,000 wire filters; the actor's
    92	working set explodes; relay workers can't send fast enough.
    93	
    94	**Setup.** Pre-generate 10,000 unique pubkeys. Mix of operations:
    95	5,000 `open_author` + 5,000 `claim_profile` in a 1-s burst, then
    96	5,000 `close_author` + 5,000 `release_profile` over the next 1 s.
    97	Repeat 5 × at 30-s intervals (5 storms total).
    98	
    99	**Threading.** Caller bursts; actor compiles; relay workers
   100	serialize REQ frames.
   101	
   102	**Assertions.**
   103	1. Peak working-set memory during storm ≤ **150 MB**.
   104	2. Wire-subscription dedup: subscriber-planner REQ frame count on
   105	   the wire ≤ **2,000** (4× compaction floor).
   106	3. After all closes, planner state size returns to baseline ± **5 %**.
   107	4. No actor-thread stall > **50 ms** during storm.
   108	5. Auto-close mechanism operates correctly under storm.
   109	
   110	**Runner.** Rust harness only — this exercises planner internals.
   111	
   112	**Numeric gate.** See gates.md §G-S8.
   113	
   114	---
   115	
   116	## S9. Relay flap — simulated WebSocket disconnect/reconnect storm
   117	
   118	**The bug shape.** Each disconnect-reconnect leaks a wire
   119	subscription; bandwidth balloons because every reconnect re-issues
   120	all subs without dedup; the planner doesn't reconcile state.
   121	
   122	**Setup.** Use the harness's mock relay (forked from
   123	`crates/nmp-testing` — see harness.md §1.5). Kill and restore the
   124	relay connection at **100 cycles per minute for 10 minutes** (1,000
   125	flaps total). Maintain a fixed open-view set: 1 timeline + 5 authors
   126	+ 2 threads = 8 logical views throughout.
   127	
   128	**Threading.** Mock relay (test harness); relay-worker reconnect
   129	loop (`run_relay_worker` in `relay_worker.rs`); actor.
   130	
   131	**Assertions.**
   132	1. Wire-subscription count after each reconnect = **8** (no growth).
   133	2. Total bytes-RX over 10 min bounded by **2 × baseline bandwidth**.
   134	3. Reconnect p99 latency from disconnect-detection to first
   135	   re-issued REQ ≤ **500 ms**.
   136	4. `reconnect_count` field in `RelayStatus` matches injection count.
   137	5. No `OutboundMessage` silently lost; deferred queue drains on reconnect.
   138	
   139	**Runner.** Rust harness primary (mock relay), XCUITest secondary
   140	(nightly only).
   141	
   142	**Numeric gate.** See gates.md §G-S9.
   143	
   144	---
   145	
   146	## S10. Long suspend simulation — 60-second background
   147	
   148	**Status: conditional on M3 (event store) + M4 (sync watermarks).**
   149	This scenario is specified now and **scheduled to land in M10.5
   150	only if M3+M4 are complete by then.** If not, S10 graduates to
   151	M11.5 and the M10.5 gate excludes it. S10 is **not** used as
   152	doctrine sign-off evidence for M10.5 (see gates.md §D1 note).
   153	
   154	**The bug shape.** iOS suspends the app for 60 s (background). On
   155	resume, the kernel actor's main loop has paused; relay sockets
   156	have timed out; sync watermarks need to drive the catch-up. If the
   157	watermark logic is wrong, the app over-fetches (bandwidth waste) or
   158	under-fetches (missed events).
   159	
   160	**Setup.** Open kernel, drive 20 events/sec for 30 s to establish
   161	baseline watermarks. Inject a 60-s synthetic main-loop pause via
   162	`SIGSTOP` on the actor thread (Rust harness only). On resume:
   163	1. Verify watermark-driven REQ uses `since = last_event_at_ms`.
   164	2. Verify replay completes within **5 s** of resume.
   165	3. Verify state reconciles to the same snapshot a never-suspended
   166	   run would produce (byte-equality on the relevant view payloads).
   167	
   168	**Threading.** Actor (suspended); relay workers (sockets time out
   169	during pause); listener (idle during pause).
   170	
   171	**Assertions.** (Conditional on M3+M4)
   172	1. Watermark `last_event_at_ms` persists across suspend.
   173	2. Catch-up REQ uses `since` correctly (no full re-fetch).
   174	3. Bandwidth over the 5-s catch-up window ≤ **3 × steady-state
   175	   bandwidth**.
   176	4. Final state snapshot identical to the non-suspended control run.
   177	
   178	**Runner.** Rust harness only (XCUITest cannot SIGSTOP the actor).
   179	
   180	**Numeric gate.** See gates.md §G-S10.
   181	
   182	---
   183	
   184	## S11. Memory RSS instrumentation — iPhone 12 baseline
   185	
   186	*This scenario is the M1 RSS-measurement deferral (T22) being honored in M10.5.*
   187	
   188	**The bug shape.** Profile-thrashing and mount/unmount-churn scenarios (S1, S2) assert
   189	RSS bounds but M1 shipped without OS-level instrumentation to measure them. Without a
   190	real RSS reading, the bounds are aspirational, not gated.
   191	
   192	**Setup.** On iPhone 12 hardware (not simulator — the baseline is device-specific), run
   193	the canonical workflow in three phases via the Instruments command-line runner:
   194	
   195	1. **Cold start.** Boot app, load seed timeline (pablof7z + fiatjaf + jb55 union),
   196	   let it stabilise for 30 s. Record RSS at stabilisation.
   197	2. **10-minute sustained scroll.** Automate continuous scroll at ≈ 3 items/sec
   198	   (XCUITest `swipe` loop) keeping roughly 100 profile views live at any moment.
   199	   Sample RSS every 5 s via `mach_task_basic_info.resident_size`.
   200	3. **Post-shutdown delta.** Stop the kernel (simulate app background), wait 10 s,
   201	   resume and read RSS again.
   202	
   203	**Instrumentation.** `mach_task_basic_info` via a thin Swift shim injected into
   204	`NmpStress`; readings appended to the in-memory metrics channel already present in
   205	`KernelMetrics`. Linux CI runs the equivalent via `/proc/self/status VmRSS` in the
   206	Rust harness for regression detection.
   207	
   208	**Threading.** Main thread runs XCUITest scroll driver; actor + relay workers run
   209	normally; the RSS probe fires on a 5-s `DispatchSourceTimer` on a background serial queue.
   210	
   211	**Assertions.**
   212	1. RSS at 100 active views ≤ **100 MB** on simulator / **150 MB** on iPhone 12 (ADR-0003
   213	   working-set gate of ≤ 100 MB at 100 active views / 10k hot events; iPhone 12 gets
   214	   50 MB headroom for OS overhead per G-S11).
   215	2. RSS growth slope over the 10-min scroll ≤ **0 bytes/sec** post-warmup (first 30 s).
   216	3. Post-shutdown RSS delta ≤ **10 MB** (no retained-by-cycle leak surviving shutdown).
   217	4. Instruments → Leaks count = 0 throughout.
   218	
   219	**Output.** RSS time-series appended to `docs/perf/m10.5/iphone12-baseline.md`
   220	alongside the rest of the iPhone 12 firehose-bench numbers.
   221	
   222	**Runner.** XCUITest on iPhone 12 hardware (mandatory); Rust harness Linux-side for
   223	CI regression tracking.
   224	
   225	**Numeric gate.** See gates.md §G-S11.

 succeeded in 0ms:
     1	# FFI hardening — exit gates (§5) and doctrine review (§8)
     2	
     3	Two tables in this file:
     4	1. **§G** numeric exit gates per scenario per metric — the complete
     5	   contract; every cell is a specific number with units. No "fast
     6	   enough", no "acceptable".
     7	2. **§D** doctrine D0–D5 review checklist — each line item maps to
     8	   a scenario, an audit document, or an explicit code-comment task.
     9	
    10	The values here are the **simulator** baseline (iPhone 16 Pro
    11	simulator on Apple Silicon Mac). The iPhone 12 hardware baseline is
    12	quoted as a separate column where scaling matters; the per-device
    13	coefficient is documented in
    14	`docs/perf/m10.5/iphone12-baseline.md` (produced by the M1–M10
    15	firehose-bench rerun, not by this design doc).
    16	
    17	---
    18	
    19	## §G. Numeric exit gates
    20	
    21	### G-S1. Mount/unmount churn (10 min)
    22	
    23	| Metric | Op | Sim threshold | iPhone 12 threshold |
    24	|---|---|---|---|
    25	| Wall duration | `==` | 600 s ± 5 s | 600 s ± 5 s |
    26	| Cycles completed | `>=` | 540,000 (90 % of 600k nominal) | 360,000 (60 %) |
    27	| Final per-pubkey refcount | `==` | 0 for every key | 0 |
    28	| Unmatched claim/release | `==` | 0 | 0 |
    29	| Process RSS growth | `<=` | 5 MiB | 8 MiB |
    30	| Counting allocator slope (post-warmup) | `<=` | 0 bytes/sec | 0 bytes/sec |
    31	| Instruments retained-by-cycle leaks | `==` | 0 | 0 |
    32	| Listener thread CPU avg | `<=` | 5 % | 8 % |
    33	
    34	### G-S2. Dispatch flood (60 s, 10 000/s, 4 threads)
    35	
    36	| Metric | Op | Sim threshold | iPhone 12 threshold |
    37	|---|---|---|---|
    38	| Dispatches submitted | `>=` | 600,000 | 600,000 |
    39	| Per-dispatch Swift→Rust send latency p50 | `<=` | 100 µs | 200 µs |
    40	| Per-dispatch Swift→Rust send latency p99 | `<=` | 1 ms | 2 ms |
    41	| Actor mpsc backlog peak | `<=` | 10,000 | 10,000 |
    42	| Main thread hitches > 16 ms (XCTHitchMetric) | `==` | 0 | 0 |
    43	| Dropped messages (Sender::send failures) | `==` | 0 | 0 |
    44	| RSS growth | `<=` | 20 MiB | 30 MiB |
    45	
    46	### G-S3. Snapshot pressure (100 k events, 10 emits)
    47	
    48	| Metric | Op | Sim threshold | iPhone 12 threshold |
    49	|---|---|---|---|
    50	| Per-emit JSON serialization wall-time p99 (Rust) | `<=` | 20 ms | 40 ms |
    51	| Per-emit payload size | `<=` | 2 MiB | 2 MiB |
    52	| Swift `apply_us` p99 | `<=` | 16 ms (16 000 µs) | 32 ms |
    53	| End-to-end reconciler frequency | `<=` | 60 Hz | 60 Hz |
    54	| Allocations per emit | `<=` | `2 × payload_bytes` | `2 × payload_bytes` |
    55	| Counting allocator: temporary peak during emit | `<=` | `3 × payload_bytes` | `3 × payload_bytes` |
    56	| `rev` monotonicity across the 10 emits | `==` | strictly increasing | strictly increasing |
    57	
    58	### G-S4. Reconciler back-pressure (12 stalls × 250 ms, 60 s)
    59	
    60	| Metric | Op | Sim threshold | iPhone 12 threshold |
    61	|---|---|---|---|
    62	| Stalls injected | `==` | 12 | 12 |
    63	| Actor `actor_queue_depth` peak during any stall | `<=` | 50 | 100 |
    64	| Listener `update_rx` backlog after each stall | `<=` | `ceil(0.25 × emit_hz) + 1` | same |
    65	| Rev order on resume | `==` | strictly monotonic | strictly monotonic |
    66	| Stale-rev filter drops counted | `>=` | 1 per stall (12 total) | same |
    67	| Total emits dropped (listener-side) | `==` | 0 | 0 |
    68	| Apply-after-resume burst max | `<=` | 33 ms | 50 ms |
    69	
    70	### G-S5. Reentrancy (30 s, 50 events/s, callback dispatches)
    71	
    72	| Metric | Op | Sim threshold | iPhone 12 threshold |
    73	|---|---|---|---|
    74	| Deadlocks (5 s watchdog) | `==` | 0 | 0 |
    75	| Dispatch-from-callback emits processed | `>=` | 100 (over 30 s) | 100 |
    76	| Out-of-order callback→dispatch pairs | `==` | 0 | 0 |
    77	| Listener thread CPU per emit avg | `<=` | 2 ms | 4 ms |
    78	| Dispatch loss | `==` | 0 | 0 |
    79	
    80	### G-S6. Capability lifecycle storms (1 000 cycles, 5 min)
    81	
    82	| Metric | Op | Sim threshold | iPhone 12 threshold |
    83	|---|---|---|---|
    84	| Cycles completed | `==` | 1,000 | 1,000 |
    85	| Thread count delta from baseline | `<=` | 2 | 2 |
    86	| Active `RelayControl` entries after final Stop | `==` | 0 | 0 |
    87	| Relay-worker generation counter wrap | `==` | 0 (no wrap) | 0 |
    88	| RSS growth | `<=` | 2 MiB | 4 MiB |
    89	| Cycle wall-time p99 | `<=` | 250 ms | 500 ms |
    90	| Deadlocks (5 s per-cycle watchdog) | `==` | 0 | 0 |
    91	
    92	### G-S7. Error-shape exhaustion (full matrix)
    93	
    94	| Metric | Op | Sim threshold | iPhone 12 threshold |
    95	|---|---|---|---|
    96	| Crashes / signals across full input matrix | `==` | 0 | 0 |
    97	| Crashes on NULL app pointer calls | `==` | 0 | 0 |
    98	| Toast field populated on every silent-no-op path | `==` | 100 % of validation-fail paths | 100 % |
    99	| Toast strings non-empty + actionable (regex match) | `==` | 100 % | 100 % |
   100	| Instruments-Allocations delta over full matrix | `==` | 0 (no leak per error path) | 0 |
   101	| Symbols × invalid-input variants exercised | `>=` | 70 (14 symbols × 5 variants avg) | 70 |
   102	
   103	### G-S8. Subscription planner DOS (5 × 10 k storm, 30 s gap)
   104	
   105	| Metric | Op | Sim threshold | iPhone 12 threshold |
   106	|---|---|---|---|
   107	| Peak working-set RSS during storm | `<=` | 150 MiB | 200 MiB |
   108	| Wire-REQ frame count per 10 k OpenViews | `<=` | 2,500 (4× dedup floor) | 2,500 |
   109	| Planner state size after all closes | `<=` | baseline × 1.05 | baseline × 1.10 |
   110	| Actor stalls > 50 ms during storm | `==` | 0 | 0 |
   111	| Auto-close success rate (every release → close) | `==` | 100 % | 100 % |
   112	| Wire subscription leaks after 5 storms | `==` | 0 | 0 |
   113	
   114	### G-S9. Relay flap (100/min × 10 min = 1 000 flaps)
   115	
   116	| Metric | Op | Sim threshold | iPhone 12 threshold |
   117	|---|---|---|---|
   118	| Flaps injected | `==` | 1,000 | 1,000 |
   119	| Wire-subscription count after each reconnect | `==` | 8 (one per logical view) | 8 |
   120	| `reconnect_count` field matches injected count | `==` | 1,000 | 1,000 |
   121	| Reconnect→first-REQ-out p99 latency | `<=` | 500 ms | 1,000 ms |
   122	| Total bytes-RX over 10 min | `<=` | 2 × baseline bandwidth | 2 × baseline |
   123	| Deferred-outbound queue drain on reconnect | `==` | 100 % | 100 % |
   124	| Subscription leaks (count after final reconnect) | `==` | 0 | 0 |
   125	
   126	### G-S10. Long suspend (conditional on M3+M4)
   127	
   128	| Metric | Op | Sim threshold | iPhone 12 threshold |
   129	|---|---|---|---|
   130	| Watermark persisted across suspend | `==` | true | true |
   131	| Catch-up REQ uses `since` filter | `==` | true | true |
   132	| Catch-up window wall-time | `<=` | 5 s | 8 s |
   133	| Catch-up bandwidth vs steady-state | `<=` | 3× | 3× |
   134	| Post-resume state snapshot equals control | `==` | byte-equal (relevant payloads) | byte-equal |
   135	
   136	### G-S11. Memory RSS instrumentation — iPhone 12 baseline
   137	
   138	*M1 RSS-measurement deferral honored here (T22). Simulator column is the Linux Rust-harness
   139	CI regression check; iPhone 12 column is the load-bearing gate.*
   140	
   141	| Metric | Op | Sim threshold (Linux Rust harness) | iPhone 12 threshold |
   142	|---|---|---|---|
   143	| RSS at stabilisation (100 active views) | `<=` | 100 MiB (ADR-0003 working-set gate) | 150 MiB |
   144	| RSS growth slope post-warmup (first 30 s) | `<=` | 0 bytes/sec | 0 bytes/sec |
   145	| Post-shutdown RSS delta | `<=` | 5 MiB | 10 MiB |
   146	| Instruments → Leaks count | `==` | 0 | 0 |
   147	| RSS sample interval | `==` | 5 s | 5 s |
   148	
   149	---
   150	
   151	## §G.1 Aggregate gates (apply across all scenarios)
   152	
   153	| Metric | Op | Threshold | Source |
   154	|---|---|---|---|
   155	| Total Instruments-Leaks across the suite | `==` | 0 | every scenario with `--instruments` |
   156	| Total crashes / panics across the suite | `==` | 0 | every scenario |
   157	| M1–M10 firehose-bench rerun regression (p99 ms, any scenario) | `<=` | +5 % vs baseline | plan.md §M10.5 exit-gate row 2 |
   158	| Pre-merge runtime budget per PR | `<=` | 15 min wall-time on `macos-14` | ci.md §1 |
   159	| Nightly runtime budget on Mac mini self-hosted | `<=` | 90 min | ci.md §2 |
   160	
   161	---
   162	
   163	## §D. Doctrine D0–D5 review checklist
   164	
   165	Cardinal doctrines per `docs/product-spec/overview-and-dx.md` §1.5.
   166	Each line item names the artifact that proves it.
   167	
   168	> **Note.** The task brief mentioned "D0–D5". The canonical list in
   169	> the spec **is exactly six items: D0, D1, D2, D3, D4, D5.** This
   170	> file follows that list. (The aim.md §6 list of 12 items is a
   171	> larger doctrine set the FFI hardening pass deliberately does
   172	> not redundantly re-prove — items beyond D0–D5 are covered by
   173	> earlier milestones' own gates.)
   174	
   175	### D0. Kernel never grows app nouns
   176	
   177	- ✅ **Proof:** [debt-inventory.md §3 D0 audit](../../perf/m10.5/debt-inventory.md) — verified
   178	  no app-domain types in `nmp-core` substrate.
   179	- ✅ **Stress proof:** S6 (capability lifecycle storms) does
   180	  1 000 start/stop/restart cycles; the kernel's capability set is
   181	  unchanged across all cycles (no dynamic registration of new
   182	  noun types).
   183	- 📝 **Sign-off:** auditor signature line in
   184	  `docs/perf/m10.5/doctrine-review.md` § D0.
   185	
   186	### D1. Best-effort rendering — render now, refine in place
   187	
   188	- ✅ **Stress proof:** S3 (snapshot pressure) — every emit must
   189	  satisfy the placeholder-then-refine contract: missing display
   190	  names → shortened-npub placeholders are present (no `None`); when
   191	  kind:0 arrives, the next emit updates in place. Assertion: for
   192	  every author with placeholder text in emit N, emit M > N where
   193	  the kind:0 arrived must contain the resolved text and an
   194	  unchanged `id`.
   195	- ✅ **Stress proof (back-pressure path):** S4 (reconciler
   196	  back-pressure) — on stall release, the timeline renders with
   197	  placeholders immediately, not spinner-blocked. Emit ordering is
   198	  monotonic; no frame is dropped.
   199	- ✅ **Code proof:** iOS `ProfileCard.placeholder(pubkey:)` renders
   200	  immediately from `debt-inventory.md` §3 D1 audit; no spinner, no
   201	  `nil` guard blocking render.
   202	- 📝 **Sign-off:** doctrine-review.md § D1.
   203	
   204	> **Note on S10.** S10 (long suspend) would provide additional D1
   205	> evidence for the resume path, but S10 is conditional on M3+M4
   206	> (persistence + watermarks), which are not complete at M10.5 close.
   207	> S10 is deferred to M11.5 or whenever M3+M4 land; it is not used as
   208	> doctrine sign-off evidence here. D1 is signed off on S3 + S4 + code
   209	> proof above.
   210	
   211	### D2. Reactivity contract — composite reverse index, ≤60Hz/view, working-set bound
   212	
   213	- ✅ **Stress proof:** S2 (dispatch flood), S3 (snapshot pressure),
   214	  S8 (planner DOS) all assert reconciler frequency stays bounded
   215	  (currently capped at 12 Hz; the doctrine says ≤ 60 Hz; the gate
   216	  is the 60 Hz ceiling, the configured cap is internal policy).
   217	- ✅ **Stress proof (working set):** S8 asserts planner state
   218	  returns to baseline ± 5 % after close storms.
   219	- ✅ **Composite reverse index:** S8 asserts wire-REQ dedup
   220	  (4× compaction floor); the reverse index drives the dedup.
   221	- 📝 **Sign-off:** doctrine-review.md § D2.
   222	
   223	### D3. Errors never cross FFI
   224	
   225	- ⚠️ **Current state:** debt-inventory §3 D3 audit concludes
   226	  errors-as-data crosses FFI correctly via `RelayStatus.last_error`
   227	  / `last_notice`. App-action error paths are
   228	  **silently no-op** (see parent doc §7.2).
   229	- ✅ **Stress proof + remediation:** S7 (error-shape exhaustion)
   230	  exercises every invalid-input path. The M10.5 deliverable
   231	  adds a `toast: Option<String>` field to the JSON update
   232	  payload and populates it from S7's failure paths. The
   233	  schema change is additive and non-breaking.
   234	- 📝 **Sign-off:** doctrine-review.md § D3, with the explicit
   235	  note that this milestone *closes* the D3-incomplete state
   236	  identified in the debt inventory.
   237	
   238	### D4. Single writer per fact — caches derive
   239	
   240	- ✅ **Proof:** debt-inventory §3 D4 audit — single-threaded actor
   241	  is the only writer; `KernelModel` on the iOS side is
   242	  `@MainActor`-isolated so derived state has a single writer per
   243	  layer.
   244	- ✅ **Stress proof:** S5 (reentrancy) — the reentrant
   245	  dispatch-from-callback path does not violate single-writer
   246	  because the dispatch enqueues a command for the actor; the
   247	  callback does not mutate kernel state directly.
   248	- ✅ **Stress proof:** S1 (mount/unmount) — refcount table is
   249	  only mutated by the actor; verified by harness asserting no
   250	  refcount-table mutation on any other thread (via
   251	  `loom`-style instrumentation in the harness binary only, not
   252	  prod).
   253	- 📝 **Sign-off:** doctrine-review.md § D4.
   254	
   255	### D5. Capabilities report, never decide
   256	
   257	Canonical (plan.md:9): capabilities surface position events to the
   258	iOS layer; **no policy decisions are made at the bridge**.
   259	
   260	- ✅ **Code proof:** `CapabilityModule` trait (`substrate/capability.rs`)
   261	  defines typed `Request`/`Result` pairs — modules *report* capability
   262	  results back to the platform; they never decide what to do with them.
   263	  The `callback_interface_name()` entry point delivers results to the
   264	  iOS layer as data, not as control signals.
   265	- ✅ **Capability evidence (M10.5 surface):** The relay-role capability
   266	  (content + indexer) is the only active module today. `RelayStatus` is
   267	  emitted as an update field — the kernel reports relay position; iOS
   268	  renders it. No routing decisions are made at the bridge.
   269	- ✅ **M11 prep evidence:** Both `AudioPlaybackCapability` and
   270	  `EmbeddingCapability` (planned for M11) follow the same pattern: the
   271	  kernel emits position/ready events back to the platform; the platform
   272	  renders them. No capability module will acquire routing authority.
   273	- ✅ **Stress proof:** S6 (capability lifecycle storms) — 1,000
   274	  start/stop/restart cycles verify the relay capability lifecycle without
   275	  any module gaining decision authority; all `RelayControl` entries are
   276	  closed after every Stop.
   277	- 📝 **Sign-off:** doctrine-review.md § D5.
   278	
   279	---
   280	
   281	## §D.1 Doctrine sign-off artifact
   282	
   283	`docs/perf/m10.5/doctrine-review.md` is produced at the *end* of
   284	M10.5, not as part of this design. The structure is:
   285	
   286	```
   287	# M10.5 Doctrine Review
   288	
   289	| Doctrine | Status | Evidence | Reviewer | Date |
   290	|---|---|---|---|---|
   291	| D0 | PASS | debt-inventory §3 D0 + S6 metrics.json | <name> | <date> |
   292	| D1 | PASS | S3 + S4 metrics.json + S3/screenshots (placeholder-then-refine path) | <name> | <date> |
   293	| D2 | PASS | S2/S3/S8 metrics.json | <name> | <date> |
   294	| D3 | PASS | S7 metrics.json + toast-bridge merge SHA | <name> | <date> |
   295	| D4 | PASS | debt-inventory §3 D4 + S5/S1 metrics.json | <name> | <date> |
   296	| D5 | PASS | debt-inventory §3 D5 + S6 metrics.json + capability.rs code review | <name> | <date> |
   297	
   298	## Notes
   299	<any caveats, deferrals, follow-ups>
   300	```
   301	
   302	A PASS in every row + the §7.1 grep gate yielding 0 hits = M10.5
   303	ready to close. Anything less = the milestone is open.

 succeeded in 0ms:
     1	# FFI hardening — run + reporting protocol (§6) and CI integration (§9)
     2	
     3	Two layers in this file:
     4	1. **§R** local run protocol — `just stress` recipes, output bundle layout.
     5	2. **§C** CI integration — pre-merge / nightly / on-demand tiers.
     6	
     7	---
     8	
     9	## §R. Run + reporting protocol
    10	
    11	### R.1 `justfile` recipes
    12	
    13	Append to `/Users/pablofernandez/Work/nostr-multi-platform/justfile`:
    14	
    15	```make
    16	# === FFI hardening (M10.5) ===
    17	
    18	# Pre-merge fast suite: simulator only, ~10 min wall-time
    19	stress:
    20	    cargo build --release -p nmp-testing --bin ffi-stress
    21	    just stress-rust-fast
    22	    just stress-xcui-fast
    23	
    24	stress-rust-fast:
    25	    cargo run --release -p nmp-testing --bin ffi-stress -- \
    26	        mount-unmount --duration 60s --fail-on-gate
    27	    cargo run --release -p nmp-testing --bin ffi-stress -- \
    28	        dispatch-flood --duration 30s --threads 4 --fail-on-gate
    29	    cargo run --release -p nmp-testing --bin ffi-stress -- \
    30	        snapshot-pressure --duration 30s --fail-on-gate
    31	    cargo run --release -p nmp-testing --bin ffi-stress -- \
    32	        reentrancy --duration 30s --fail-on-gate
    33	    cargo run --release -p nmp-testing --bin ffi-stress -- \
    34	        error-exhaustion --fail-on-gate
    35	    cargo run --release -p nmp-testing --bin ffi-stress -- \
    36	        planner-dos --duration 60s --fail-on-gate
    37	
    38	stress-xcui-fast: build-ios
    39	    xcodebuild test \
    40	        -project ios/NmpStress/NmpStress.xcodeproj \
    41	        -scheme StressUITests-Fast \
    42	        -destination 'platform=iOS Simulator,name=iPhone 16 Pro,OS=latest' \
    43	        -derivedDataPath ios/DerivedData \
    44	        -resultBundlePath docs/perf/m10.5/xcui-fast.xcresult
    45	
    46	# Nightly full suite: all scenarios at full duration, includes device
    47	stress-full:
    48	    just stress-rust-full
    49	    just stress-xcui-full
    50	    just stress-device
    51	
    52	stress-rust-full:
    53	    cargo run --release -p nmp-testing --bin ffi-stress -- \
    54	        all --duration 10m --instruments --fail-on-gate
    55	
    56	stress-xcui-full: build-ios
    57	    xcodebuild test \
    58	        -project ios/NmpStress/NmpStress.xcodeproj \
    59	        -scheme StressUITests-Full \
    60	        -destination 'platform=iOS Simulator,name=iPhone 16 Pro,OS=latest' \
    61	        -derivedDataPath ios/DerivedData \
    62	        -resultBundlePath docs/perf/m10.5/xcui-full.xcresult
    63	
    64	stress-device:
    65	    cargo build --release -p nmp-core --target aarch64-apple-ios
    66	    xcodebuild test \
    67	        -project ios/NmpStress/NmpStress.xcodeproj \
    68	        -scheme StressUITests-Full \
    69	        -destination 'platform=iOS,name=iPhone 12' \
    70	        -derivedDataPath ios/DerivedData \
    71	        -resultBundlePath docs/perf/m10.5/xcui-device.xcresult
    72	
    73	stress-sonnet:
    74	    just build-ios
    75	    crates/nmp-testing/bin/ffi-stress/sonnet-runner.sh \
    76	        default 4 5
    77	
    78	# Aggregate all scenario JSON into one markdown summary
    79	stress-report:
    80	    python3 scripts/stress-aggregate.py docs/perf/m10.5/ \
    81	        > docs/perf/m10.5/m10.5-summary.md
    82	
    83	# Pre-merge gate: fast scenarios only; no doctrine review required
    84	stress-gate-fast:
    85	    python3 scripts/stress-gate.py docs/perf/m10.5/ \
    86	        --scenarios S1,S2,S3,S5,S7,S8
    87	
    88	# Milestone-close gate: full battery + doctrine review + debt clean
    89	stress-close-gate:
    90	    python3 scripts/stress-gate.py docs/perf/m10.5/ \
    91	        --require-doctrines D0,D1,D2,D3,D4,D5 \
    92	        --require-debt-inventory-clean \
    93	        --require-grep-clean
    94	```
    95	
    96	### R.2 Output bundle layout
    97	
    98	Every run produces (or appends to) a tree rooted at
    99	`docs/perf/m10.5/`:
   100	
   101	```
   102	docs/perf/m10.5/
   103	├── README.md                       # top-level index, auto-generated
   104	├── debt-inventory.md               # produced by debt-auditor (exists 2026-05-18)
   105	├── doctrine-review.md              # M10.5 sign-off, produced at gate close
   106	├── iphone12-baseline.md            # firehose-bench rerun on real hardware
   107	├── m10.5-summary.md                # aggregate of all scenarios, auto-generated
   108	├── S1/
   109	│   ├── report.md                   # human-readable, per-metric pass/fail
   110	│   ├── metrics.json                # schema in harness.md §4
   111	│   ├── instruments.trace/          # only if --instruments was passed
   112	│   │   └── ... (xctrace bundle)
   113	│   ├── screenshots/                # XCUITest screenshots, time-ordered
   114	│   │   ├── 0001-start.png
   115	│   │   ├── 0002-mid-churn-1min.png
   116	│   │   └── ...
   117	│   └── raw/
   118	│       ├── ffi-stress.log          # stdout from Rust harness
   119	│       └── xctrace.log             # stdout from xctrace
   120	├── S2/ ...
   121	├── S3/ ...
   122	├── ... (one dir per scenario through S10)
   123	└── sonnet/
   124	    └── <scenario>-<unix-ts>/       # per-run, multiple permitted
   125	        ├── agent-1/
   126	        │   ├── transcript.json
   127	        │   ├── assertions.log
   128	        │   └── screenshots/
   129	        ├── agent-2/ ...
   130	        └── aggregate-report.md
   131	```
   132	
   133	### R.3 Report content (`<scenario>/report.md`)
   134	
   135	Generated by the harness; example for S1:
   136	
   137	```markdown
   138	# S1 — Mount/unmount churn — 2026-06-XX
   139	
   140	| Gate | Threshold | Measured | Result |
   141	|---|---|---|---|
   142	| Wall duration | == 600 s ± 5 s | 600.3 s | ✅ |
   143	| Cycles completed | >= 540,000 | 591,217 | ✅ |
   144	| Final per-pubkey refcount | == 0 | 0 | ✅ |
   145	| RSS growth | <= 5 MiB | 1.8 MiB | ✅ |
   146	| Instruments retained-cycle leaks | == 0 | 0 | ✅ |
   147	| Counting allocator slope (post-warmup) | <= 0 bytes/sec | -240 bytes/sec | ✅ |
   148	
   149	**Overall:** PASS (6/6 gates green)
   150	
   151	## Observations
   152	- First 30 s: allocator slope +3.2 KiB/sec during warmup; expected.
   153	- After cycle 100 000: slope drops to -240 bytes/sec (gradual reclamation of fragmented allocations).
   154	- Instruments-Leaks bundle: see ./instruments.trace.
   155	
   156	## Limitations
   157	- Sim only; iPhone 12 device rerun pending (S1 on device: see docs/perf/m10.5/S1-iphone12-2026-06-XX/report.md).
   158	```
   159	
   160	### R.4 The gate script
   161	
   162	`scripts/stress-gate.py` is the source of truth. It supports two
   163	modes, controlled by CLI flags:
   164	
   165	**Fast mode (`stress-gate-fast` — pre-merge):** only fast simulator
   166	scenarios. No doctrine review. No debt-inventory check.
   167	
   168	**Close mode (`stress-close-gate` — milestone close):** full battery
   169	plus doctrine review, debt-inventory, and grep gate.
   170	
   171	Pseudocode:
   172	
   173	```python
   174	def gate(perf_dir, scenarios=None, require_doctrines=None,
   175	         require_debt_clean=False, require_grep_clean=False):
   176	    fails = []
   177	    # Fast mode: ["S1","S2","S3","S5","S7","S8"]
   178	    # Close mode: ["S1","S2","S3","S5","S6","S7","S8","S9"]
   179	    # S4 is XCUITest-only; S10 deferred until M3+M4 complete
   180	    for scenario in (scenarios or ["S1","S2","S3","S5","S6","S7","S8","S9"]):
   181	        m = load(f"{perf_dir}/{scenario}/metrics.json")
   182	        if not m["passed"]:
   183	            fails.append(f"{scenario}: {failed_gates(m)}")
   184	
   185	    if require_doctrines:
   186	        d = load(f"{perf_dir}/doctrine-review.md")
   187	        for code in require_doctrines:
   188	            if not has_passing_row(d, code):
   189	                fails.append(f"doctrine {code} not signed off")
   190	
   191	    if require_debt_clean:
   192	        debt = load(f"{perf_dir}/debt-inventory.md")
   193	        if has_open_must_fix(debt):
   194	            fails.append("debt-inventory open must-fix items")
   195	
   196	    if require_grep_clean:
   197	        if grep_ffi_surface_for_debt_tokens() != 0:
   198	            fails.append("FFI grep yielded TODO/FIXME tokens; see §7.1")
   199	
   200	    return (len(fails) == 0, fails)
   201	```
   202	
   203	Exit 0 = gate passes. Non-zero = work remaining; failures printed.
   204	
   205	---
   206	
   207	CI tier workflows (pre-merge, nightly, on-demand, trace replay,
   208	artifact retention, and the M10.5 close protocol) are in
   209	[`ci-tiers.md`](./ci-tiers.md).

 succeeded in 0ms:
     1	# FFI hardening — scenarios (§3)
     2	
     3	Eleven named failure modes. Each entry: setup, assertion, threading target,
     4	runner (Rust harness / XCUITest / Sonnet-agent), and numeric threshold.
     5	Numeric thresholds are quoted here for clarity; the canonical exit-gate
     6	table lives in [`gates.md`](./gates.md).
     7	
     8	Conventions:
     9	- "dispatch" = a single Swift→Rust call through any `nmp_app_*` symbol.
    10	- "emit" = one full update payload delivered to the registered callback.
    11	- "view-handle wrapper" = the iOS-side refcounted entity created when a
    12	  SwiftUI component mounts a `useProfile(pubkey)`-style observer (in the
    13	  current `NmpStress` codebase this maps to `KernelModel.claimProfile` +
    14	  `releaseProfile`; ADR-0005).
    15	
    16	---
    17	
    18	## S1. Mount/unmount churn — view-handle wrapper refcount
    19	
    20	**The bug shape.** Wrappers (`claim_profile` / `release_profile`) leak
    21	internal references; the kernel's per-pubkey refcount table grows
    22	without bound; eventually the platform shadow holds entries no
    23	component still needs.
    24	
    25	**Setup.** Drive the kernel with a closed-loop of 1,000
    26	claim/release pairs per second across 100 unique pubkeys (rotating
    27	LRU). Sustain for **10 minutes** (600,000 cycles). Each cycle:
    28	`claim_profile(pk_i, consumer_id_i) → 1ms wait → release_profile(pk_i, consumer_id_i)`.
    29	
    30	**Threading.** Driver thread = Swift main (XCUITest) **or** the
    31	single-threaded executor in the Rust harness. Actor thread receives
    32	the storm; listener emits batched updates.
    33	
    34	**Assertions.**
    35	1. Final refcount for every pubkey = 0.
    36	2. `Kernel::interest_refcount(pk).is_none()` for every pubkey after a
    37	   grace-period (configurable, default 30 s — ADR-0005).
    38	3. Process RSS after 10 min ≤ baseline + **5 MB**.
    39	4. Instruments → Leaks → 0 retained-by-cycle leaks.
    40	5. Allocator (counting allocator from `reactivity-bench`) shows
    41	   slope ≤ 0 bytes/sec post-warmup (first 30 s).
    42	
    43	**Runner.** Primary: Rust harness `ffi-stress mount-unmount-churn`.
    44	Secondary (nightly only): XCUITest `S1MountUnmountChurn.swift` driving
    45	real `NmpStress` with Instruments-Leaks attached.
    46	
    47	**Numeric gate.** See gates.md §G-S1.
    48	
    49	---
    50	
    51	## S2. Dispatch flood
    52	
    53	**The bug shape.** Swift dispatches faster than the actor can drain;
    54	the mpsc channel grows unbounded; OOM eventually. Or: the FFI symbol
    55	itself takes a lock that blocks the main thread.
    56	
    57	**Setup.** **10,000 dispatches per second** for 60 s across **N=4**
    58	caller threads (matching iOS's typical concurrency budget). Mix:
    59	30 % `open_author` (valid pubkey from a pool of 50), 30 %
    60	`close_author`, 20 % `claim_profile`, 20 % `release_profile`. All
    61	inputs valid (no validation-path flooding — that's S7).
    62	
    63	**Threading.** Four caller OS threads (in the Rust harness, four
    64	`std::thread`; in the XCUITest variant, four `DispatchQueue.global()`
    65	queues). Actor thread is the bottleneck under test.
    66	
    67	**Assertions.**
    68	1. No dispatch call takes > **1 ms** at p99 (Swift→Rust channel send
    69	   latency; this is the bible-#3 "fire-and-forget" guarantee
    70	   quantified).
    71	2. Main thread (XCUITest variant) is never blocked > **16 ms**
    72	   (measured via XCTest's `XCTHitchMetric`).
    73	3. Actor mpsc backlog never exceeds **10,000 messages** (configurable
    74	   harness-side check via `actor_queue_depth` field already exposed in
    75	   `KernelMetrics`).
    76	4. Zero dropped messages: count of `_ = tx.send(...)` failures = 0.
    77	5. RSS growth over 60 s ≤ 20 MB.
    78	
    79	**Runner.** Rust harness primary. XCUITest secondary because the
    80	real-world iOS main-thread budget is the load-bearing constraint.
    81	
    82	---
    83	
    84	## S3. Snapshot pressure — `AppUpdate::FullState` with 100k events
    85	
    86	**The bug shape.** Marshal cost for a full-state snapshot grows
    87	super-linearly; the listener thread spends > 1 emit-interval
    88	serializing JSON; the reconciler exceeds 60 Hz; the iOS main thread
    89	spends every frame applying snapshots.
    90	
    91	**Setup.** Pre-load kernel state via a captured firehose-bench trace
    92	(`docs/perf/firehose-bench/traces/m10.5-snapshot.cap`, captured
    93	expressly for this scenario) containing **100,000 stored events**
    94	across 3,000 authors. Force a full-state emit by calling
    95	`nmp_app_configure` (which currently triggers `emit_now`) ten times
    96	in a row to amortize the JSON cost over ten observations.
    97	
    98	**Threading.** Actor builds the update; listener serializes; Swift
    99	main applies. The bottleneck under test is the **listener
   100	serialization + main-thread apply** path.
   101	
   102	**Assertions.**
   103	1. Per-emit JSON serialization wall-time p99 ≤ **20 ms** (Rust side).
   104	2. Payload size ≤ **2 MiB** (if it exceeds, the harness fails and
   105	   asks for bible-#10 granular-update variants).
   106	3. Swift `apply_us` (already instrumented in `KernelModel.apply`) p99
   107	   ≤ **16 ms**.
   108	4. End-to-end reconciler frequency stays ≤ **60 Hz** (configured cap
   109	   is 12 Hz today; the harness verifies the cap is honored under
   110	   pressure rather than asserting the cap value itself).
   111	5. Allocations per emit ≤ **payload_bytes × 2** (no quadratic-copy
   112	   regression).
   113	
   114	**Runner.** Rust harness primary (measures Rust-side); XCUITest
   115	secondary (measures Swift-side).
   116	
   117	---
   118	
   119	## S4. Reconciler back-pressure — main thread stalled 250 ms
   120	
   121	**The bug shape.** When the iOS main thread blocks (e.g.,
   122	file-picker, modal sheet, large layout pass), the listener-thread
   123	emits accumulate. On resume, the app applies a flood and visibly
   124	hitches, or worse, the actor stalls because its `update_tx` channel
   125	fills.
   126	
   127	**Setup.** Start the kernel, open following + author + thread views,
   128	drive 100 events/sec for 60 s. Periodically inject a **250 ms
   129	synchronous sleep on the main thread** (XCUITest:
   130	`Thread.sleep(forTimeInterval: 0.25)` inside the reconciler closure).
   131	Repeat every 5 s for the 60-s window → **12 stalls total**.
   132	
   133	**Threading.** Main thread (artificially stalled); listener thread
   134	(accumulates emits); actor (must not block on `update_tx.send`).
   135	
   136	**Assertions.**
   137	1. Actor `actor_queue_depth` never grows during a stall (the listener
   138	   thread is the queue under back-pressure, not the actor's command
   139	   queue).
   140	2. Listener `update_rx` backlog after each 250 ms stall ≤ ⌈ 250 ms
   141	   × emit_hz ⌉ + 1 = **2** messages (at 4 Hz default) or **4** (at 12
   142	   Hz max).
   143	3. On stall release, the main thread applies all backlogged emits in
   144	   monotonic `rev` order (bible #1).
   145	4. Stale-rev filter in `KernelModel.apply` correctly drops any
   146	   intermediate revs (already implemented at KernelModel.swift:139;
   147	   harness validates).
   148	5. No emit is dropped — the listener delivers each one in order.
   149	
   150	**Runner.** XCUITest only (the bug shape is iOS-main-thread specific).
   151	
   152	---
   153	
   154	## S5. Reentrancy — dispatch from inside reconciler callback
   155	
   156	**The bug shape.** A SwiftUI `.onChange` handler observes a kernel
   157	update and immediately calls `kernel.openAuthor(...)`. If the FFI
   158	symbol takes a lock the listener thread also takes, deadlock. If it
   159	re-enters synchronously, message ordering can invert.
   160	
   161	**Setup.** Register a callback that, on every emit where
   162	`update.metrics.eventsSinceLastUpdate > 0`, immediately dispatches
   163	`open_author` for the first item in the timeline. Sustain for 30 s
   164	with a 50 events/sec inflow.
   165	
   166	**Threading.** Callback runs on **listener thread**; dispatch
   167	enqueues to the actor's command channel which the **actor thread**
   168	drains. The bug shape requires the actor to be processing a message
   169	when the callback fires (race window).
   170	
   171	**Assertions.**
   172	1. Zero deadlocks (harness times out after 5 s = fail).
   173	2. Message order preserved — for every emit-then-dispatch pair, the
   174	   resulting `OpenAuthor` action is processed strictly **after** the
   175	   emit that triggered it (verified via `rev` monotonicity in the
   176	   subsequent emit referencing that author view).
   177	3. No dispatch loss under reentrant pressure.
   178	4. Listener thread CPU time per emit ≤ **2 ms** even with the
   179	   reentrant dispatch path active.
   180	
   181	**Runner.** Rust harness primary, XCUITest secondary.
   182	
   183	---
   184	
   185	Scenarios S6–S11 (capability storms, error exhaustion, planner DOS,
   186	relay flap, long suspend, memory RSS instrumentation) are in
   187	[`scenarios-detail.md`](./scenarios-detail.md).

    25	# === FFI hardening (M10.5 phase 1) ===
    26	# Runs S1..S5 Rust harness scenarios against nmp_app_* C symbols.
    27	# Per-scenario output: docs/perf/m10.5/<SCENARIO>/{metrics.json,report.md}
    28	
    29	# Individual scenario shortcuts.
    30	stress-s1:
    31	    cargo run --release -p nmp-testing --bin ffi-stress -- mount-unmount --write-report --fail-on-gate
    32	
    33	stress-s2:
    34	    cargo run --release -p nmp-testing --bin ffi-stress -- dispatch-flood --write-report --fail-on-gate
    35

    38

    41	
    42	stress-s5:
    43	    cargo run --release -p nmp-testing --bin ffi-stress -- reentrancy --write-report --fail-on-gate
    44	
    45	# Generic dispatcher: `just stress s1` .. `just stress s5`
    46	stress S:
    47	    cargo run --release -p nmp-testing --bin ffi-stress -- {{S}} --write-report --fail-on-gate
    48	
    49	# Pre-merge fast gate: S1+S2+S3+S5 at fast durations (S4 is XCUITest-primary;
    50	# S7/S8 are out of scope for phase 1). Target: < 5 min wall-time.
    51	# Per docs/design/ffi-hardening/ci.md §9.
    52	stress-gate-fast:
    53	    cargo run --release -p nmp-testing --bin ffi-stress -- mount-unmount --duration 60s --write-report --fail-on-gate
    54	    cargo run --release -p nmp-testing --bin ffi-stress -- dispatch-flood --duration 30s --threads 4 --write-report --fail-on-gate

 succeeded in 0ms:
     1	//! S1 — Mount/unmount churn (view-handle wrapper refcount).
     2	//!
     3	//! Spec: docs/design/ffi-hardening/scenarios.md §S1
     4	//! Gate: docs/design/ffi-hardening/gates.md §G-S1
     5	//!
     6	//! Drives 1,000 claim/release pairs/sec across 100 unique pubkeys
     7	//! for the configured duration (full: 600 s; fast: 60 s).
     8	//!
     9	//! D4 (single writer per fact): refcount table mutated on actor thread only.
    10	//! D8 (zero per-event allocations after warmup): counting allocator checks
    11	//!    that heap slope post-warmup is <= 0.
    12	
    13	use crate::allocator::{alloc_snapshot, AllocSnapshot};
    14	use crate::ffi::{
    15	    nmp_app_claim_profile, nmp_app_configure, nmp_app_free, nmp_app_new, nmp_app_release_profile,
    16	    nmp_app_set_update_callback, process_rss_bytes, test_pubkeys, NmpApp,
    17	};
    18	use crate::gate::Gate;
    19	use crate::report::ScenarioMetrics;
    20	use serde_json::json;
    21	use std::ffi::{c_char, c_void, CString};
    22	use std::sync::atomic::{AtomicU64, Ordering};
    23	use std::time::{Duration, Instant};
    24	
    25	/// Shared callback counter — we only need to know the listener is alive.
    26	static CALLBACK_COUNT: AtomicU64 = AtomicU64::new(0);
    27	
    28	extern "C" fn sink_cb(_ctx: *mut c_void, _payload: *const c_char) {
    29	    CALLBACK_COUNT.fetch_add(1, Ordering::Relaxed);
    30	}
    31	
    32	/// Scenario configuration.
    33	pub(crate) struct S1Config {
    34	    /// How long to run (600 s full / 60 s fast).
    35	    pub(crate) duration: Duration,
    36	    /// How many distinct pubkeys to rotate across (spec: 100).
    37	    pub(crate) pool_size: usize,
    38	    /// Target claim+release pairs per second (spec: 1,000).
    39	    pub(crate) pairs_per_sec: u64,
    40	}
    41	
    42	impl Default for S1Config {
    43	    fn default() -> Self {
    44	        S1Config {
    45	            duration: Duration::from_secs(60),
    46	            pool_size: 100,
    47	            pairs_per_sec: 1000,
    48	        }
    49	    }
    50	}
    51	
    52	/// Run S1. Returns the completed `ScenarioMetrics` ready for serialization.
    53	pub(crate) fn run(cfg: S1Config, report: &mut ScenarioMetrics) {
    54	    let wall_start = Instant::now();
    55	
    56	    // --- Setup ---
    57	    // Configure-not-Start: nmp_app_configure sets emit_hz/visible_limit without spawning
    58	    // relay worker threads that would attempt TCP connections to wss://relay.primal.net.
    59	    // S1 tests FFI dispatch latency and refcount correctness, not relay connectivity.
    60	    let app: *mut NmpApp = nmp_app_new();
    61	    nmp_app_set_update_callback(app, std::ptr::null_mut(), Some(sink_cb));
    62	    nmp_app_configure(app, 0, 80, 4);
    63	
    64	    let pubkeys = test_pubkeys(cfg.pool_size);
    65	    // Stable consumer IDs: in production, a consumer ID is a view lifecycle token
    66	    // (e.g. "timeline-view"), not a per-cycle unique string.  Use a small fixed pool
    67	    // of 8 consumers rotated independently from the pubkey pool so every
    68	    // (pubkey, consumer) pair is exercised without per-cycle allocations.
    69	    let consumers: Vec<CString> = (0..8)
    70	        .map(|i| CString::new(format!("ffi-stress-consumer-{i}")).expect("no nuls"))
    71	        .collect();
    72	    let baseline_rss = process_rss_bytes();
    73	
    74	    // Warmup: 30 s or 5 % of duration (whichever is smaller).
    75	    let warmup = Duration::from_secs(30).min(cfg.duration / 20);
    76	    let warmup_end = Instant::now() + warmup;
    77	
    78	    // The 1ms wait inside fire_cycle (claim → release) IS the inter-pair interval.
    79	    // At 1000 pairs/sec the pairs are back-to-back: claim(t=0ms) → wait 1ms →
    80	    // release(t=1ms) → next claim(t=1ms). No outer sleep needed; adding one would
    81	    // halve throughput to ~500 pairs/sec. We spin tightly and let the inner sleep
    82	    // handle pacing.
    83	    let mut cycles: u64 = 0;
    84	
    85	    // --- Warmup phase ---
    86	    while Instant::now() < warmup_end {
    87	        fire_cycle(app, &pubkeys, &consumers, cycles);
    88	        cycles += 1;
    89	    }
    90	
    91	    // --- Steady-state phase (allocator measurement) ---
    92	    let ss_snap_before: AllocSnapshot = alloc_snapshot();
    93	    let ss_start = Instant::now();
    94	    let mut ss_cycles: u64 = 0;
    95	
    96	    while wall_start.elapsed() < cfg.duration {
    97	        fire_cycle(app, &pubkeys, &consumers, cycles);
    98	        cycles += 1;
    99	        ss_cycles += 1;
   100	    }
   101	
   102	    let ss_elapsed = ss_start.elapsed().as_secs_f64();
   103	    let ss_snap_after = alloc_snapshot();
   104	
   105	    // Capture run time before teardown; nmp_app_free joins threads and can
   106	    // add several seconds that are not part of the scenario wall time.
   107	    let run_elapsed = wall_start.elapsed().as_secs_f64();
   108	
   109	    // --- Teardown: clear callback before freeing ---
   110	    nmp_app_set_update_callback(app, std::ptr::null_mut(), None);
   111	    nmp_app_free(app);
   112	
   113	    // --- Metrics ---
   114	    let wall_elapsed = run_elapsed;
   115	    let final_rss = process_rss_bytes();
   116	    let rss_growth_bytes = final_rss.saturating_sub(baseline_rss);
   117	    let nominal_cycles = cfg.pairs_per_sec * cfg.duration.as_secs();
   118	    // macOS sleep(1ms) timer resolution: actual sleep is ~1.5 ms on average, limiting
   119	    // achievable throughput to ~670 pairs/sec rather than the spec's 1000/sec.
   120	    // The cycles gate verifies the scenario ran without stalling, not that it hit the
   121	    // theoretical maximum.  Gate at 60 % of nominal (= measured ~670/sec × 0.9 / 1000)
   122	    // to pass on macOS while still catching severe stall regressions (< 600/sec).
   123	    let min_cycles = nominal_cycles * 60 / 100;
   124	
   125	    // Net heap slope (bytes/sec) in steady state — D8 invariant.
   126	    // Use net_heap_delta (live bytes) not bytes_since (gross allocations) so that
   127	    // transient per-cycle allocations (CString, etc.) that are immediately freed
   128	    // do not fail the D8 gate; only RETAINED growth fails.
   129	    let net_heap_delta = ss_snap_after.net_heap_delta(&ss_snap_before);
   130	    let net_heap_slope = if ss_elapsed > 0.0 { net_heap_delta as f64 / ss_elapsed } else { 0.0 };
   131	    // Keep gross bytes for informational measurement.
   132	    let ss_bytes = ss_snap_after.bytes_since(&ss_snap_before);
   133	
   134	    // G-S1 gates
   135	    // RSS gate: spec says <= 5 MiB for XCUITest (production dispatch rates).
   136	    // The Rust harness drives at ~670 pairs/sec × 2 emit_now/cycle which inflates
   137	    // the listener channel and RSS vs the iOS production rate. Gate at 10 MiB here
   138	    // (2× spec) to separate harness-rate RSS inflation from actual per-cycle leaks.
   139	    report.gates.push(
   140	        Gate::lte(
   141	            "rss_growth_bytes",
   142	            rss_growth_bytes as f64,
   143	            10.0 * 1024.0 * 1024.0,
   144	        )
   145	        .with_note("G-S1/harness: RSS growth <= 10 MiB (2× spec; accounts for harness emit rate)"),
   146	    );
   147	    report.gates.push(
   148	        Gate::gte("cycles_completed", cycles as f64, min_cycles as f64).with_note(
   149	            "G-S1: cycles completed >= 60% of nominal (macOS sleep(1ms) ≈ 1.5ms → ~670/sec max)",
   150	        ),
   151	    );
   152	    report.gates.push(
   153	        Gate::lte("net_heap_slope_bytes_per_sec", net_heap_slope, 0.0).with_note(
   154	            "G-S1/D8: net heap slope <= 0 bytes/sec post-warmup (transient allocs excluded)",
   155	        ),
   156	    );
   157	
   158	    // Wall time gate: within 5 s of target
   159	    let target_secs = cfg.duration.as_secs_f64();
   160	    report.gates.push(
   161	        Gate::lte("wall_seconds_over", (wall_elapsed - target_secs).max(0.0), 5.0)
   162	            .with_note("G-S1: wall duration == target ± 5 s"),
   163	    );
   164	
   165	    report.notes.push(format!(
   166	        "Warmup duration: {:.1} s; steady-state cycles: {}; callback fires: {}",
   167	        warmup.as_secs_f64(),
   168	        ss_cycles,
   169	        CALLBACK_COUNT.load(Ordering::Relaxed)
   170	    ));
   171	    report.notes.push(
   172	        "Claim/release pairing verified structurally: every cycle fires both calls in order. \
   173	         Kernel-side refcount audit requires T23 test-support feature (phase 2)."
   174	            .to_string(),
   175	    );
   176	    if net_heap_slope > 0.0 {
   177	        report.notes.push(format!(
   178	            "D8 FINDING: net_heap_slope = {net_heap_slope:.0} bytes/sec > 0. \
   179	             Root cause: dispatch_command calls emit_now() unconditionally after every \
   180	             claim/release (actor/mod.rs). At {:.0} pairs/sec × 2 emit_now/cycle = {:.0} \
   181	             full JSON serialisations/sec; the listener channel accumulates messages faster \
   182	             than it can drain them. Fix: apply the flush_due() rate-limit gate inside \
   183	             dispatch_command for non-critical commands (profile claim/release), or batch \
   184	             claims at the FFI boundary. D8 contract (≤ 0 bytes/sec) is not met at this \
   185	             dispatch rate; gate correctly reports FAIL.",
   186	            cycles as f64 / run_elapsed,
   187	            cycles as f64 / run_elapsed * 2.0,
   188	        ));
   189	    }
   190	
   191	    report.measurements = json!({
   192	        "cycles_total": cycles,
   193	        "cycles_steady_state": ss_cycles,
   194	        "nominal_cycles": nominal_cycles,
   195	        "rss_growth_bytes": rss_growth_bytes,
   196	        "net_heap_slope_bytes_per_sec": net_heap_slope,
   197	        "net_heap_delta_bytes": net_heap_delta,
   198	        "ss_allocs": ss_snap_after.allocs_since(&ss_snap_before),
   199	        "ss_gross_bytes_allocated": ss_bytes,
   200	        "wall_seconds": wall_elapsed,
   201	        "callback_count": CALLBACK_COUNT.load(Ordering::Relaxed),
   202	    });
   203	
   204	    report.finish(wall_elapsed);
   205	}
   206	
   207	fn fire_cycle(app: *mut NmpApp, pubkeys: &[std::ffi::CString], consumers: &[CString], cycle: u64) {
   208	    // Rotate pubkey and consumer independently so every (pubkey, consumer) pair
   209	    // is exercised, but both are stable strings from a small fixed pool.
   210	    // In production, consumer IDs are view lifecycle tokens (e.g. "timeline-view"),
   211	    // not per-cycle unique strings; unique-per-cycle IDs cause spurious HashMap churn
   212	    // that is not representative of production memory pressure.
   213	    let pk = &pubkeys[cycle as usize % pubkeys.len()];
   214	    let consumer = &consumers[cycle as usize % consumers.len()];
   215	    nmp_app_claim_profile(app, pk.as_ptr(), consumer.as_ptr());
   216	    // 1 ms between claim and release per spec.
   217	    std::thread::sleep(Duration::from_millis(1));
   218	    nmp_app_release_profile(app, pk.as_ptr(), consumer.as_ptr());
   219	}

 succeeded in 0ms:
     1	//! S2 — Dispatch flood (mpsc backpressure).
     2	//!
     3	//! Spec: docs/design/ffi-hardening/scenarios.md §S2
     4	//! Gate: docs/design/ffi-hardening/gates.md §G-S2
     5	//!
     6	//! 10,000 dispatches/sec from N=4 caller threads × 60 s.
     7	//! Mix: 30% open_author, 30% close_author, 20% claim_profile, 20% release_profile.
     8	//!
     9	//! D2 (<=60 Hz reactivity bound): actor mpsc backlog never exceeds 10,000.
    10	//! Bible #3 (fire-and-forget): every send call returns within p99 <= 1 ms.
    11	
    12	use crate::ffi::{
    13	    nmp_app_claim_profile, nmp_app_close_author, nmp_app_configure, nmp_app_free, nmp_app_new,
    14	    nmp_app_open_author, nmp_app_release_profile, nmp_app_set_update_callback, process_rss_bytes,
    15	    test_pubkeys, NmpApp,
    16	};
    17	use crate::gate::Gate;
    18	use crate::report::ScenarioMetrics;
    19	use serde_json::json;
    20	use std::ffi::{c_char, c_void, CString};
    21	use std::sync::atomic::{AtomicU64, Ordering};
    22	use std::sync::{Arc, Barrier};
    23	use std::time::{Duration, Instant};
    24	
    25	static CALLBACK_COUNT: AtomicU64 = AtomicU64::new(0);
    26	
    27	extern "C" fn sink_cb(_ctx: *mut c_void, _payload: *const c_char) {
    28	    CALLBACK_COUNT.fetch_add(1, Ordering::Relaxed);
    29	}
    30	
    31	pub(crate) struct S2Config {
    32	    /// Wall duration (spec: 60 s).
    33	    pub(crate) duration: Duration,
    34	    /// Caller thread count (spec: 4).
    35	    pub(crate) threads: usize,
    36	    /// Total dispatches per second across all threads (spec: 10,000).
    37	    pub(crate) dispatches_per_sec: u64,
    38	    /// Pubkey pool size (spec: 50).
    39	    pub(crate) pool_size: usize,
    40	}
    41	
    42	impl Default for S2Config {
    43	    fn default() -> Self {
    44	        S2Config {
    45	            duration: Duration::from_secs(30),
    46	            threads: 4,
    47	            dispatches_per_sec: 10_000,
    48	            pool_size: 50,
    49	        }
    50	    }
    51	}
    52	
    53	pub(crate) fn run(cfg: S2Config, report: &mut ScenarioMetrics) {
    54	    let wall_start = Instant::now();
    55	
    56	    // Configure-not-Start: nmp_app_configure sets emit_hz/visible_limit without spawning
    57	    // relay worker threads. S2 floods open_author/close_author at 10k/sec; spawning relay
    58	    // workers would send 3k+ REQ/CLOSE per second to real external relays, filling the TCP
    59	    // write buffer and blocking relay threads indefinitely — causing a hang at teardown.
    60	    let app: *mut NmpApp = nmp_app_new();
    61	    nmp_app_set_update_callback(app, std::ptr::null_mut(), Some(sink_cb));
    62	    nmp_app_configure(app, 0, 80, 4);
    63	
    64	    let baseline_rss = process_rss_bytes();
    65	
    66	    // Convert raw pointer to usize for Send-safe sharing across threads.
    67	    let app_usize = app as usize;
    68	    let pubkeys_arc: Arc<Vec<CString>> = Arc::new(test_pubkeys(cfg.pool_size));
    69	
    70	    // Per-thread rate: total / threads.
    71	    let per_thread_dps = cfg.dispatches_per_sec / cfg.threads as u64;
    72	    let interval_ns = 1_000_000_000u64 / per_thread_dps.max(1);
    73	    let duration = cfg.duration;
    74	    let threads = cfg.threads;
    75	
    76	    // Barrier so all threads start dispatching simultaneously.
    77	    let barrier = Arc::new(Barrier::new(cfg.threads));
    78	
    79	    // Collect per-thread latency samples.
    80	    let latency_collector: Arc<std::sync::Mutex<Vec<u64>>> =
    81	        Arc::new(std::sync::Mutex::new(Vec::new()));
    82	    let dispatch_counter: Arc<AtomicU64> = Arc::new(AtomicU64::new(0));
    83	
    84	    let handles: Vec<_> = (0..cfg.threads)
    85	        .map(|thread_idx| {
    86	            let barrier = Arc::clone(&barrier);
    87	            let pubkeys = Arc::clone(&pubkeys_arc);
    88	            let latencies = Arc::clone(&latency_collector);
    89	            let counter = Arc::clone(&dispatch_counter);
    90	
    91	            std::thread::spawn(move || {
    92	                let app_ptr = app_usize as *mut NmpApp;
    93	                barrier.wait();
    94	                let thread_start = Instant::now();
    95	                let mut local_latencies: Vec<u64> = Vec::with_capacity(1024);
    96	                let mut seq: u64 = thread_idx as u64;
    97	                let mut next_tick = Instant::now();
    98	
    99	                while thread_start.elapsed() < duration {
   100	                    let pk_idx = seq as usize % pubkeys.len();
   101	                    let pk = &pubkeys[pk_idx];
   102	                    // Mix per spec: 30/30/20/20.
   103	                    let dispatch_kind = seq % 10;
   104	
   105	                    let t0 = Instant::now();
   106	                    match dispatch_kind {
   107	                        0..=2 => nmp_app_open_author(app_ptr, pk.as_ptr()),
   108	                        3..=5 => nmp_app_close_author(app_ptr, pk.as_ptr()),
   109	                        6..=7 => {
   110	                            let consumer =
   111	                                CString::new(format!("t{thread_idx}-{seq}")).expect("no nuls");
   112	                            nmp_app_claim_profile(app_ptr, pk.as_ptr(), consumer.as_ptr());
   113	                        }
   114	                        _ => {
   115	                            let consumer =
   116	                                CString::new(format!("t{thread_idx}-{seq}")).expect("no nuls");
   117	                            nmp_app_release_profile(app_ptr, pk.as_ptr(), consumer.as_ptr());
   118	                        }
   119	                    }
   120	                    let elapsed_ns = t0.elapsed().as_nanos() as u64;
   121	                    local_latencies.push(elapsed_ns);
   122	                    counter.fetch_add(1, Ordering::Relaxed);
   123	                    seq += threads as u64;
   124	
   125	                    next_tick += Duration::from_nanos(interval_ns);
   126	                    if let Some(sleep) = next_tick.checked_duration_since(Instant::now()) {
   127	                        std::thread::sleep(sleep);
   128	                    }
   129	                }
   130	
   131	                latencies.lock().unwrap().extend(local_latencies);
   132	            })
   133	        })
   134	        .collect();
   135	
   136	    for handle in handles {
   137	        let _ = handle.join();
   138	    }
   139	
   140	    let wall_elapsed = wall_start.elapsed().as_secs_f64();
   141	    let final_rss = process_rss_bytes();
   142	    let rss_growth_bytes = final_rss.saturating_sub(baseline_rss);
   143	    let total_dispatches = dispatch_counter.load(Ordering::Relaxed);
   144	
   145	    // Compute latency percentiles.
   146	    let mut latencies = latency_collector.lock().unwrap().clone();
   147	    latencies.sort_unstable();
   148	    let p50_ns = percentile(&latencies, 50);
   149	    let p99_ns = percentile(&latencies, 99);
   150	
   151	    // G-S2 numeric gates.
   152	    let p99_ms = p99_ns as f64 / 1_000_000.0;
   153	    let p50_ms = p50_ns as f64 / 1_000_000.0;
   154	    let nominal = cfg.dispatches_per_sec * cfg.duration.as_secs();
   155	
   156	    // Threshold scales with configured duration: 90 % of nominal (dispatches_per_sec × seconds).
   157	    let min_dispatches = cfg.dispatches_per_sec * cfg.duration.as_secs() * 90 / 100;
   158	    report.gates.push(
   159	        Gate::gte("dispatches_submitted", total_dispatches as f64, min_dispatches as f64)
   160	            .with_note("G-S2: dispatches_submitted >= 90% of nominal (600k full / 270k fast)"),
   161	    );
   162	    report.gates.push(
   163	        Gate::lte("send_latency_p99_ms", p99_ms, 1.0)
   164	            .with_note("G-S2/bible#3: Swift->Rust send latency p99 <= 1 ms"),
   165	    );
   166	    report.gates.push(
   167	        Gate::lte("send_latency_p50_ms", p50_ms, 0.1)
   168	            .with_note("G-S2: Swift->Rust send latency p50 <= 100 us"),
   169	    );
   170	    report.gates.push(
   171	        Gate::lte(
   172	            "rss_growth_bytes",
   173	            rss_growth_bytes as f64,
   174	            20.0 * 1024.0 * 1024.0,
   175	        )
   176	        .with_note("G-S2: RSS growth <= 20 MiB over 60 s"),
   177	    );
   178	
   179	    report.notes.push(format!(
   180	        "Nominal dispatches: {}; actual: {}; p50={:.3}ms p99={:.3}ms",
   181	        nominal, total_dispatches, p50_ms, p99_ms,
   182	    ));
   183	    report.notes.push(
   184	        "Actor mpsc backlog depth: not directly observable from caller thread; \
   185	         RSS growth is the proxy gate (bounded channel growth = bounded RSS)."
   186	            .to_string(),
   187	    );
   188	
   189	    report.measurements = json!({
   190	        "total_dispatches": total_dispatches,
   191	        "nominal_dispatches": nominal,
   192	        "threads": cfg.threads,
   193	        "dispatches_per_sec": cfg.dispatches_per_sec,
   194	        "p50_ns": p50_ns,
   195	        "p99_ns": p99_ns,
   196	        "p50_ms": p50_ms,
   197	        "p99_ms": p99_ms,
   198	        "rss_growth_bytes": rss_growth_bytes,
   199	        "callback_count": CALLBACK_COUNT.load(Ordering::Relaxed),
   200	        "wall_seconds": wall_elapsed,
   201	        "latency_samples": latencies.len(),
   202	    });
   203	
   204	    // Teardown.
   205	    nmp_app_set_update_callback(app, std::ptr::null_mut(), None);
   206	    nmp_app_free(app);
   207	
   208	    report.finish(wall_elapsed);
   209	}
   210	
   211	fn percentile(sorted: &[u64], pct: usize) -> u64 {
   212	    if sorted.is_empty() {
   213	        return 0;
   214	    }
   215	    let idx = ((sorted.len() - 1) * pct) / 100;
   216	    sorted[idx]
   217	}

 succeeded in 0ms:
     1	//! S3 — Snapshot pressure (`AppUpdate::FullState` with 100k events).
     2	//!
     3	//! Spec: docs/design/ffi-hardening/scenarios.md §S3
     4	//! Gate: docs/design/ffi-hardening/gates.md §G-S3
     5	//!
     6	//! Host-side analog: stream events via open_firehose_tag to build up kernel
     7	//! state, then trigger ten forced configure() calls to stress the
     8	//! listener serialization + callback path.
     9	//!
    10	//! The exact "100k events via captured trace" path (requiring an in-process
    11	//! relay or T23 actor injection) is a phase-2 deliverable. This phase-1
    12	//! implementation drives the real ingest path via the FFI surface and
    13	//! measures the observable (callback receive-to-return latency). The gate
    14	//! for per-emit p99 JSON serialization time is measured on the callback side.
    15	//!
    16	//! D1 (best-effort rendering): every emit processed promptly without blocking.
    17	//! D2 (<=60 Hz): reconciler frequency stays bounded by configured emit_hz.
    18	
    19	use crate::ffi::{
    20	    nmp_app_configure, nmp_app_free, nmp_app_new, nmp_app_open_firehose_tag,
    21	    nmp_app_set_update_callback, process_rss_bytes, NmpApp,
    22	};
    23	use crate::gate::Gate;
    24	use crate::report::ScenarioMetrics;
    25	use serde_json::json;
    26	use std::ffi::{c_char, c_void};
    27	use std::sync::Mutex;
    28	use std::time::{Duration, Instant};
    29	
    30	/// Shared callback state for S3.
    31	struct CallbackState {
    32	    /// Callback latencies (ns).
    33	    callback_latencies_ns: Vec<u64>,
    34	    /// Payload bytes received per emit.
    35	    payload_sizes: Vec<usize>,
    36	    /// `rev` field extracted from each emit (monotonicity check).
    37	    revs: Vec<u64>,
    38	}
    39	
    40	extern "C" fn measure_cb(ctx: *mut c_void, payload: *const c_char) {
    41	    let t0 = Instant::now();
    42	
    43	    // SAFETY: ctx is a valid Box<Mutex<CallbackState>> that lives for the
    44	    // duration of this run; callback is cleared before drop.
    45	    let state_ptr = ctx as *mut Mutex<CallbackState>;
    46	    if let Ok(mut state) = unsafe { (*state_ptr).lock() } {
    47	        let payload_len = if payload.is_null() {
    48	            0
    49	        } else {
    50	            // SAFETY: payload is a valid C string for this callback's duration.
    51	            unsafe { std::ffi::CStr::from_ptr(payload) }.to_bytes().len()
    52	        };
    53	
    54	        let rev = if !payload.is_null() {
    55	            let bytes = unsafe { std::ffi::CStr::from_ptr(payload) }.to_bytes();
    56	            extract_rev_field(bytes).unwrap_or(0)
    57	        } else {
    58	            0
    59	        };
    60	
    61	        state.payload_sizes.push(payload_len);
    62	        state.revs.push(rev);
    63	
    64	        let latency = t0.elapsed().as_nanos() as u64;
    65	        state.callback_latencies_ns.push(latency);
    66	    }
    67	}
    68	
    69	/// Extract the `"rev":N` field from a JSON payload without a full parse.
    70	fn extract_rev_field(bytes: &[u8]) -> Option<u64> {
    71	    let s = std::str::from_utf8(bytes).ok()?;
    72	    let key = "\"rev\":";
    73	    let pos = s.find(key)?;
    74	    let rest = &s[pos + key.len()..];
    75	    let end = rest.find([',', '}', ' ', '\n']).unwrap_or(rest.len());
    76	    rest[..end].trim().parse::<u64>().ok()
    77	}
    78	
    79	pub(crate) struct S3Config {
    80	    /// Tag to open for ingest (any value drives the firehose path).
    81	    pub(crate) firehose_tag: String,
    82	    /// How long to drive events before triggering configure() bursts.
    83	    pub(crate) warmup_duration: Duration,
    84	    /// Number of configure() calls to trigger forced emits (spec: 10).
    85	    pub(crate) configure_bursts: usize,
    86	    /// Interval between configure() calls.
    87	    pub(crate) burst_interval: Duration,
    88	}
    89	
    90	impl Default for S3Config {
    91	    fn default() -> Self {
    92	        S3Config {
    93	            firehose_tag: "test".to_string(),
    94	            warmup_duration: Duration::from_secs(10),
    95	            configure_bursts: 10,
    96	            burst_interval: Duration::from_millis(200),
    97	        }
    98	    }
    99	}
   100	
   101	pub(crate) fn run(cfg: S3Config, report: &mut ScenarioMetrics) {
   102	    let wall_start = Instant::now();
   103	
   104	    let app: *mut NmpApp = nmp_app_new();
   105	    let baseline_rss = process_rss_bytes();
   106	
   107	    // Allocate callback state on the heap; pass raw pointer as context.
   108	    let state = Box::new(Mutex::new(CallbackState {
   109	        callback_latencies_ns: Vec::new(),
   110	        payload_sizes: Vec::new(),
   111	        revs: Vec::new(),
   112	    }));
   113	    let ctx = Box::into_raw(state) as *mut c_void;
   114	
   115	    nmp_app_set_update_callback(app, ctx, Some(measure_cb));
   116	    // Configure-not-Start: no relay workers needed; S3 tests emit-rate gating,
   117	    // not relay connectivity. nmp_app_configure sets emit_hz/visible_limit only.
   118	    // 12 Hz emit cap ensures we can measure frequency vs the 60 Hz bound.
   119	    nmp_app_configure(app, 0, 500, 12);
   120	
   121	    // Open firehose to drive ingest.
   122	    let tag = std::ffi::CString::new(cfg.firehose_tag.as_str()).expect("no nuls");
   123	    nmp_app_open_firehose_tag(app, tag.as_ptr());
   124	
   125	    // Let events accumulate during warmup.
   126	    std::thread::sleep(cfg.warmup_duration);
   127	
   128	    // Trigger ten configure() calls to force serialization bursts.
   129	    let burst_start = Instant::now();
   130	    for _ in 0..cfg.configure_bursts {
   131	        nmp_app_configure(app, 0, 500, 12);
   132	        std::thread::sleep(cfg.burst_interval);
   133	    }
   134	    let burst_elapsed = burst_start.elapsed();
   135	
   136	    // Allow one more tick for the final emits to arrive.
   137	    std::thread::sleep(Duration::from_millis(500));
   138	
   139	    let wall_elapsed = wall_start.elapsed().as_secs_f64();
   140	    let final_rss = process_rss_bytes();
   141	
   142	    // Teardown — clear callback before reclaiming ctx.
   143	    nmp_app_set_update_callback(app, std::ptr::null_mut(), None);
   144	    nmp_app_free(app);
   145	
   146	    // SAFETY: callback is cleared; no other references to ctx remain.
   147	    let state = unsafe { Box::from_raw(ctx as *mut Mutex<CallbackState>) };
   148	    let state = state.lock().unwrap();
   149	
   150	    let rss_growth = final_rss.saturating_sub(baseline_rss);
   151	    let emit_count = state.callback_latencies_ns.len();
   152	
   153	    // Callback latency percentiles (approximates Rust-side emit cost).
   154	    let mut lats = state.callback_latencies_ns.clone();
   155	    lats.sort_unstable();
   156	    let p99_cb_ns = percentile_u64(&lats, 99);
   157	    let p99_cb_ms = p99_cb_ns as f64 / 1_000_000.0;
   158	
   159	    // Payload size gate.
   160	    let max_payload = state.payload_sizes.iter().copied().max().unwrap_or(0);
   161	
   162	    // Rev monotonicity check.
   163	    let revs_monotonic = is_strictly_increasing(&state.revs);
   164	
   165	    // Emit frequency over burst window.
   166	    let burst_hz = if burst_elapsed.as_secs_f64() > 0.0 {
   167	        emit_count as f64 / burst_elapsed.as_secs_f64()
   168	    } else {
   169	        0.0
   170	    };
   171	
   172	    // G-S3 gates.
   173	    report.gates.push(
   174	        Gate::lte("callback_p99_ms", p99_cb_ms, 20.0)
   175	            .with_note("G-S3: per-emit JSON serialization wall-time p99 <= 20 ms (Rust-side)"),
   176	    );
   177	    report.gates.push(
   178	        Gate::lte(
   179	            "max_payload_bytes",
   180	            max_payload as f64,
   181	            2.0 * 1024.0 * 1024.0,
   182	        )
   183	        .with_note("G-S3: per-emit payload size <= 2 MiB"),
   184	    );
   185	    report.gates.push(
   186	        Gate::lte("emit_hz", burst_hz, 60.0)
   187	            .with_note("G-S3: end-to-end reconciler frequency <= 60 Hz"),
   188	    );
   189	    report.gates.push(
   190	        Gate::eq("rev_monotonic", if revs_monotonic { 1.0 } else { 0.0 }, 1.0)
   191	            .with_note("G-S3: rev field strictly increasing across emits (bible #1)"),
   192	    );
   193	
   194	    report.notes.push(format!(
   195	        "Phase-1 host approximation: events driven via open_firehose_tag (no relay). \
   196	         Full 100k snapshot test requires T23 actor-injection (phase 2). \
   197	         Emits observed: {emit_count}; burst window: {:.1} s; burst Hz: {:.1}",
   198	        burst_elapsed.as_secs_f64(),
   199	        burst_hz
   200	    ));
   201	
   202	    report.measurements = json!({
   203	        "emit_count": emit_count,
   204	        "configure_bursts": cfg.configure_bursts,
   205	        "burst_elapsed_ms": burst_elapsed.as_millis(),
   206	        "burst_hz": burst_hz,
   207	        "p99_callback_ns": p99_cb_ns,
   208	        "p99_callback_ms": p99_cb_ms,
   209	        "max_payload_bytes": max_payload,
   210	        "rss_growth_bytes": rss_growth,
   211	        "rev_monotonic": revs_monotonic,
   212	        "wall_seconds": wall_elapsed,
   213	    });
   214	
   215	    report.finish(wall_elapsed);
   216	}
   217	
   218	fn percentile_u64(sorted: &[u64], pct: usize) -> u64 {
   219	    if sorted.is_empty() {
   220	        return 0;
   221	    }
   222	    let idx = ((sorted.len() - 1) * pct) / 100;
   223	    sorted[idx]
   224	}
   225	
   226	fn is_strictly_increasing(revs: &[u64]) -> bool {
   227	    if revs.len() < 2 {
   228	        return true;
   229	    }
   230	    // Allow zeros (unparsed) at the start — only check parsed pairs.
   231	    let non_zero: Vec<u64> = revs.iter().copied().filter(|&r| r > 0).collect();
   232	    if non_zero.len() < 2 {
   233	        return true;
   234	    }
   235	    non_zero.windows(2).all(|w| w[1] > w[0])
   236	}
