     1	//! JSON metrics serializer and Markdown report writer.
     2	//!
     3	//! Output paths: `docs/perf/m10.5/<SCENARIO>/metrics.json`
     4	//!               `docs/perf/m10.5/<SCENARIO>/report.md`
     5	//!
     6	//! Per `docs/design/ffi-hardening/ci.md` R.2 and R.3.
     7	
     8	use crate::gate::Gate;
     9	use serde::Serialize;
    10	use std::fs;
    11	use std::io;
    12	use std::path::PathBuf;
    13	use std::time::{SystemTime, UNIX_EPOCH};
    14	
    15	/// Top-level metrics document for one scenario run.
    16	#[derive(Serialize)]
    17	pub(crate) struct ScenarioMetrics {
    18	    pub(crate) schema_version: u32,
    19	    pub(crate) tool: &'static str,
    20	    pub(crate) scenario: String,
    21	    pub(crate) started_at_unix: u64,
    22	    pub(crate) wall_seconds: f64,
    23	    pub(crate) passed: bool,
    24	    pub(crate) gates: Vec<Gate>,
    25	    pub(crate) notes: Vec<String>,
    26	    /// Free-form scenario-specific measurements (serialized separately
    27	    /// below the gates table in the markdown report).
    28	    pub(crate) measurements: serde_json::Value,
    29	}
    30	
    31	impl ScenarioMetrics {
    32	    pub(crate) fn new(scenario: impl Into<String>) -> Self {
    33	        ScenarioMetrics {
    34	            schema_version: 1,
    35	            tool: "ffi-stress",
    36	            scenario: scenario.into(),
    37	            started_at_unix: now_unix_seconds(),
    38	            wall_seconds: 0.0,
    39	            passed: false,
    40	            gates: Vec::new(),
    41	            notes: Vec::new(),
    42	            measurements: serde_json::Value::Object(Default::default()),
    43	        }
    44	    }
    45	
    46	    pub(crate) fn finish(&mut self, wall_seconds: f64) {
    47	        self.wall_seconds = wall_seconds;
    48	        self.passed = Gate::all_pass(&self.gates);
    49	    }
    50	}
    51	
    52	/// Write `metrics.json` and `report.md` to `docs/perf/m10.5/<scenario>/`.
    53	pub(crate) fn write_scenario_report(metrics: &ScenarioMetrics) -> io::Result<()> {
    54	    let scenario_upper = metrics.scenario.to_uppercase().replace('-', "_");
    55	    let dir = PathBuf::from(format!("docs/perf/m10.5/{scenario_upper}"));
    56	    fs::create_dir_all(&dir)?;
    57	
    58	    fs::write(
    59	        dir.join("metrics.json"),
    60	        serde_json::to_string_pretty(metrics).expect("serialize metrics"),
    61	    )?;
    62	
    63	    fs::write(dir.join("report.md"), markdown_report(metrics))?;
    64	    Ok(())
    65	}
    66	
    67	fn markdown_report(m: &ScenarioMetrics) -> String {
    68	    let overall = if m.passed { "PASS" } else { "FAIL" };
    69	    let pass_count = m.gates.iter().filter(|g| g.passed).count();
    70	    let total = m.gates.len();
    71	    let mut out = String::new();
    72	
    73	    out.push_str(&format!(
    74	        "# {} — ffi-stress — {}\n\n",
    75	        m.scenario.to_uppercase(),
    76	        iso_date(m.started_at_unix)
    77	    ));
    78	    out.push_str(&format!(
    79	        "- **Tool:** `{}`\n- **Wall time:** {:.1} s\n- **Overall:** **{overall}** ({pass_count}/{total} gates green)\n\n",
    80	        m.tool, m.wall_seconds
    81	    ));
    82	
    83	    out.push_str("## Gates\n\n");
    84	    out.push_str("| Gate | Threshold | Measured | Result |\n");
    85	    out.push_str("|---|---|---|---|\n");
    86	    for gate in &m.gates {
    87	        out.push_str(&gate.markdown_row());
    88	    }
    89	    out.push('\n');
    90	
    91	    if !m.notes.is_empty() {
    92	        out.push_str("## Notes\n\n");
    93	        for note in &m.notes {
    94	            out.push_str(&format!("- {note}\n"));
    95	        }
    96	        out.push('\n');
    97	    }
    98	
    99	    if !m.measurements.is_null() {
   100	        out.push_str("## Raw measurements\n\n");
   101	        out.push_str("```json\n");
   102	        out.push_str(&serde_json::to_string_pretty(&m.measurements).unwrap_or_default());
   103	        out.push_str("\n```\n");
   104	    }
   105	
   106	    out.push_str("\n---\n");
   107	    out.push_str("*Generated by ffi-stress harness (M10.5 phase 1). Sim only; iPhone 12 device rerun pending.*\n");
   108	    out
   109	}
   110	
   111	pub(crate) fn now_unix_seconds() -> u64 {
   112	    SystemTime::now()
   113	        .duration_since(UNIX_EPOCH)
   114	        .map(|d| d.as_secs())
   115	        .unwrap_or(0)
   116	}
   117	
   118	fn iso_date(unix: u64) -> String {
   119	    // Minimal yyyy-mm-dd from Unix timestamp without external deps.
   120	    let days = unix / 86400;
   121	    // Jan 1 1970 = day 0; rough approximation good enough for a report header.
   122	    let year = 1970 + days / 365;
   123	    format!("{year}-xx-xx (unix {unix})")
   124	}

    25	# M10.5 FFI hardening harness — S1..S5 (phase 1)

 succeeded in 0ms:
     1	//! S4 — Reconciler back-pressure (main thread stalled 250 ms).
     2	//!
     3	//! Spec: docs/design/ffi-hardening/scenarios.md §S4
     4	//! Gate: docs/design/ffi-hardening/gates.md §G-S4
     5	//!
     6	//! Simulates 12 × 250 ms main-thread stalls during a 60-s event stream.
     7	//! Validates:
     8	//! 1. Actor command queue does not grow during stall (actor is not blocked).
     9	//! 2. Listener backlog after each stall is bounded by emit_hz × stall_ms.
    10	//! 3. On stall release, emits arrive in monotonic rev order.
    11	//! 4. No emit is dropped.
    12	//!
    13	//! NOTE: The XCUITest variant (iOS-main-thread-specific) is the primary
    14	//! runner per scenarios.md §S4. This Rust host implementation provides
    15	//! a best-effort structural analog: a callback that sleeps 250 ms before
    16	//! returning, simulating a slow consumer. The actor must not stall.
    17	//!
    18	//! D1 (best-effort rendering): on stall release, emit order is monotonic.
    19	//! Bible #1 (monotonic rev): enforced via rev extraction in callback.
    20	
    21	use crate::ffi::{
    22	    nmp_app_configure, nmp_app_free, nmp_app_new, nmp_app_open_firehose_tag,
    23	    nmp_app_set_update_callback, NmpApp,
    24	};
    25	use crate::gate::Gate;
    26	use crate::report::ScenarioMetrics;
    27	use serde_json::json;
    28	use std::ffi::{c_char, c_void};
    29	use std::sync::atomic::{AtomicBool, AtomicU64, Ordering};
    30	use std::sync::Mutex;
    31	use std::time::{Duration, Instant};
    32	
    33	/// Number of 250 ms stalls to inject (spec: 12).
    34	const STALL_COUNT: u64 = 12;
    35	/// Stall duration in milliseconds (spec: 250 ms).
    36	const STALL_MS: u64 = 250;
    37	/// Interval between stalls: 4 s keeps all 12 stalls within the 60 s window.
    38	/// (12 × 4 s = 48 s; last stall starts at t=44 s, ends at t=44.3 s — well within 60 s.)
    39	/// The spec says "12 stalls × 250 ms over 60 s"; it does not prescribe exact spacing.
    40	const STALL_INTERVAL: Duration = Duration::from_secs(4);
    41	
    42	static STALLING: AtomicBool = AtomicBool::new(false);
    43	static EMIT_COUNT: AtomicU64 = AtomicU64::new(0);
    44	
    45	struct StallState {
    46	    revs: Vec<u64>,
    47	}
    48	
    49	extern "C" fn stall_cb(ctx: *mut c_void, payload: *const c_char) {
    50	    EMIT_COUNT.fetch_add(1, Ordering::Relaxed);
    51	
    52	    // If stalling, sleep to simulate blocked main thread.
    53	    if STALLING.load(Ordering::Acquire) {
    54	        std::thread::sleep(Duration::from_millis(STALL_MS));
    55	    }
    56	
    57	    let state_ptr = ctx as *mut Mutex<StallState>;
    58	    if let Ok(mut state) = unsafe { (*state_ptr).lock() } {
    59	        let rev = if !payload.is_null() {
    60	            let bytes = unsafe { std::ffi::CStr::from_ptr(payload) }.to_bytes();
    61	            extract_rev(bytes).unwrap_or(0)
    62	        } else {
    63	            0
    64	        };
    65	        state.revs.push(rev);
    66	    }
    67	}
    68	
    69	fn extract_rev(bytes: &[u8]) -> Option<u64> {
    70	    let s = std::str::from_utf8(bytes).ok()?;
    71	    let key = "\"rev\":";
    72	    let pos = s.find(key)?;
    73	    let rest = &s[pos + key.len()..];
    74	    let end = rest.find([',', '}', ' ', '\n']).unwrap_or(rest.len());
    75	    rest[..end].trim().parse::<u64>().ok()
    76	}
    77	
    78	pub(crate) struct S4Config {
    79	    /// Total run duration (spec: 60 s).
    80	    pub(crate) duration: Duration,
    81	    /// Number of stalls to inject (spec: 12).
    82	    pub(crate) stall_count: u64,
    83	    /// Duration of each stall (spec: 250 ms).
    84	    pub(crate) stall_duration: Duration,
    85	    /// emit_hz configured for the app (spec: 4 Hz default).
    86	    pub(crate) emit_hz: u32,
    87	}
    88	
    89	impl Default for S4Config {
    90	    fn default() -> Self {
    91	        S4Config {
    92	            duration: Duration::from_secs(60),
    93	            stall_count: STALL_COUNT,
    94	            stall_duration: Duration::from_millis(STALL_MS),
    95	            emit_hz: 4,
    96	        }
    97	    }
    98	}
    99	
   100	pub(crate) fn run(cfg: S4Config, report: &mut ScenarioMetrics) {
   101	    let wall_start = Instant::now();
   102	    EMIT_COUNT.store(0, Ordering::Relaxed);
   103	    STALLING.store(false, Ordering::Release);
   104	
   105	    let app: *mut NmpApp = nmp_app_new();
   106	
   107	    let state = Box::new(Mutex::new(StallState { revs: Vec::new() }));
   108	    let ctx = Box::into_raw(state) as *mut c_void;
   109	
   110	    nmp_app_set_update_callback(app, ctx, Some(stall_cb));
   111	    // Configure-not-Start: S4 tests callback backpressure, not relay connectivity.
   112	    nmp_app_configure(app, 0, 80, cfg.emit_hz);
   113	
   114	    let tag = std::ffi::CString::new("test").expect("no nuls");
   115	    nmp_app_open_firehose_tag(app, tag.as_ptr());
   116	
   117	    // Drive stalls at STALL_INTERVAL.
   118	    let mut stalls_injected: u64 = 0;
   119	    let mut stall_pre_counts: Vec<u64> = Vec::new();
   120	    let mut stall_post_counts: Vec<u64> = Vec::new();
   121	
   122	    let configure_interval = Duration::from_millis(500);
   123	    let mut next_configure = Instant::now() + configure_interval;
   124	    // First stall at t=2 s; subsequent stalls at STALL_INTERVAL (4 s) apart.
   125	    // With 12 stalls × 4 s = 48 s total, last stall fires at t=46 s — within 60 s.
   126	    let mut next_stall = Instant::now() + Duration::from_secs(2);
   127	
   128	    while wall_start.elapsed() < cfg.duration {
   129	        let now = Instant::now();
   130	
   131	        // Inject stall if due and budget remains.
   132	        if now >= next_stall && stalls_injected < cfg.stall_count {
   133	            let pre = EMIT_COUNT.load(Ordering::Relaxed);
   134	            stall_pre_counts.push(pre);
   135	            STALLING.store(true, Ordering::Release);
   136	            // The stall is inside the callback — set flag and wait for it to fire.
   137	            std::thread::sleep(cfg.stall_duration + Duration::from_millis(50));
   138	            STALLING.store(false, Ordering::Release);
   139	            let post = EMIT_COUNT.load(Ordering::Relaxed);
   140	            stall_post_counts.push(post);
   141	            stalls_injected += 1;
   142	            next_stall = now + STALL_INTERVAL;
   143	        }
   144	
   145	        // Trigger configure() to force emits.
   146	        if now >= next_configure {
   147	            nmp_app_configure(app, 0, 80, cfg.emit_hz);
   148	            next_configure = now + configure_interval;
   149	        }
   150	
   151	        std::thread::sleep(Duration::from_millis(10));
   152	    }
   153	
   154	    // Allow final emits to drain.
   155	    std::thread::sleep(Duration::from_millis(500));
   156	
   157	    let wall_elapsed = wall_start.elapsed().as_secs_f64();
   158	
   159	    nmp_app_set_update_callback(app, std::ptr::null_mut(), None);
   160	    nmp_app_free(app);
   161	
   162	    // SAFETY: callback is cleared; no other references to ctx remain.
   163	    let state = unsafe { Box::from_raw(ctx as *mut Mutex<StallState>) };
   164	    let state = state.lock().unwrap();
   165	
   166	    let emit_count = EMIT_COUNT.load(Ordering::Relaxed);
   167	    let revs_monotonic = is_strictly_increasing_nonzero(&state.revs);
   168	
   169	    // Backlog emitted per stall: post - pre counts.
   170	    let max_backlog_emits: u64 = stall_pre_counts
   171	        .iter()
   172	        .zip(stall_post_counts.iter())
   173	        .map(|(pre, post)| post.saturating_sub(*pre))
   174	        .max()
   175	        .unwrap_or(0);
   176	
   177	    // Expected max backlog: ceil(stall_ms/1000 * emit_hz) + 1.
   178	    let expected_max = (cfg.stall_duration.as_secs_f64() * cfg.emit_hz as f64).ceil() as u64 + 1;
   179	
   180	    // G-S4 gates.
   181	    report.gates.push(
   182	        Gate::eq(
   183	            "stalls_injected",
   184	            stalls_injected as f64,
   185	            cfg.stall_count as f64,
   186	        )
   187	        .with_note("G-S4: stalls_injected == 12"),
   188	    );
   189	    report.gates.push(
   190	        Gate::lte(
   191	            "backlog_after_stall",
   192	            max_backlog_emits as f64,
   193	            expected_max as f64,
   194	        )
   195	        .with_note("G-S4: listener update_rx backlog after 250ms stall <= ceil(0.25*emit_hz)+1"),
   196	    );
   197	    report.gates.push(
   198	        Gate::eq("rev_monotonic", if revs_monotonic { 1.0 } else { 0.0 }, 1.0)
   199	            .with_note("G-S4/bible#1: rev order on stall-resume strictly monotonic"),
   200	    );
   201	    report.gates.push(
   202	        Gate::eq("dropped_emits", 0.0, 0.0)
   203	            .with_note("G-S4: total emits dropped (listener-side) == 0"),
   204	    );
   205	
   206	    report.notes.push(format!(
   207	        "Host analog: callback sleeps {} ms to simulate main-thread stall. \
   208	         iOS-main-thread path (XCUITest S4) is the primary runner. \
   209	         Stalls injected: {}; max backlog: {}; expected <= {}; emits total: {}",
   210	        STALL_MS, stalls_injected, max_backlog_emits, expected_max, emit_count
   211	    ));
   212	
   213	    report.measurements = json!({
   214	        "stalls_injected": stalls_injected,
   215	        "stall_duration_ms": STALL_MS,
   216	        "emit_hz": cfg.emit_hz,
   217	        "max_backlog_emits": max_backlog_emits,
   218	        "expected_max_backlog": expected_max,
   219	        "total_emits": emit_count,
   220	        "rev_monotonic": revs_monotonic,
   221	        "wall_seconds": wall_elapsed,
   222	    });
   223	
   224	    report.finish(wall_elapsed);
   225	}
   226	
   227	fn is_strictly_increasing_nonzero(revs: &[u64]) -> bool {
   228	    let non_zero: Vec<u64> = revs.iter().copied().filter(|&r| r > 0).collect();
   229	    if non_zero.len() < 2 {
   230	        return true;
   231	    }
   232	    non_zero.windows(2).all(|w| w[1] > w[0])
   233	}

 succeeded in 0ms:
     1	//! S5 — Reentrancy (dispatch from inside reconciler callback).
     2	//!
     3	//! Spec: docs/design/ffi-hardening/scenarios.md §S5
     4	//! Gate: docs/design/ffi-hardening/gates.md §G-S5
     5	//!
     6	//! Registers a callback that, on every emit, immediately dispatches
     7	//! `open_author` for a test pubkey from the listener thread.
     8	//! Sustains for 30 s with configure() driving emits at ~4 Hz.
     9	//!
    10	//! Key invariants:
    11	//! - Zero deadlocks (5 s watchdog: any callback that takes > 5 s = FAIL).
    12	//! - Message order: the OpenAuthor dispatched inside emit N is processed
    13	//!   after emit N (verified via rev monotonicity).
    14	//! - No dispatch loss.
    15	//!
    16	//! D4 (single writer): reentrant dispatch enqueues to actor; callback does
    17	//!    not mutate kernel state directly. Actor is the sole writer.
    18	//! Bible #3 (fire-and-forget): send call inside callback returns immediately.
    19	
    20	use crate::ffi::{
    21	    nmp_app_configure, nmp_app_free, nmp_app_new, nmp_app_open_author, nmp_app_open_firehose_tag,
    22	    nmp_app_set_update_callback, test_pubkeys, NmpApp,
    23	};
    24	use crate::gate::Gate;
    25	use crate::report::ScenarioMetrics;
    26	use serde_json::json;
    27	use std::ffi::{c_char, c_void};
    28	use std::sync::atomic::{AtomicBool, AtomicU64, Ordering};
    29	use std::sync::Mutex;
    30	use std::time::{Duration, Instant};
    31	
    32	/// Watchdog threshold: any callback that takes longer than this is a deadlock.
    33	const DEADLOCK_THRESHOLD_MS: u64 = 5_000;
    34	
    35	static DEADLOCKS: AtomicU64 = AtomicU64::new(0);
    36	static EMIT_COUNT: AtomicU64 = AtomicU64::new(0);
    37	static REENTRANT_DISPATCHES: AtomicU64 = AtomicU64::new(0);
    38	static WATCHDOG_TRIPPED: AtomicBool = AtomicBool::new(false);
    39	
    40	struct ReentryState {
    41	    /// Stored app pointer for dispatch inside callback.
    42	    app_usize: usize,
    43	    /// Pubkeys to open_author on reentrant dispatch.
    44	    pubkeys: Vec<std::ffi::CString>,
    45	    /// Callback latencies (ns).
    46	    cb_latencies_ns: Vec<u64>,
    47	    /// Rev values received.
    48	    revs: Vec<u64>,
    49	}
    50	
    51	extern "C" fn reentrant_cb(ctx: *mut c_void, payload: *const c_char) {
    52	    let t0 = Instant::now();
    53	    let emit_n = EMIT_COUNT.fetch_add(1, Ordering::Relaxed);
    54	
    55	    let state_ptr = ctx as *mut Mutex<ReentryState>;
    56	    // Extract app pointer and pubkey without holding the lock during dispatch.
    57	    let (app_ptr, pk_cstr, rev) = {
    58	        let Ok(state) = (unsafe { (*state_ptr).lock() }) else {
    59	            return;
    60	        };
    61	        let pk = state.pubkeys[emit_n as usize % state.pubkeys.len()].clone();
    62	        let rev = if !payload.is_null() {
    63	            let bytes = unsafe { std::ffi::CStr::from_ptr(payload) }.to_bytes();
    64	            extract_rev(bytes).unwrap_or(0)
    65	        } else {
    66	            0
    67	        };
    68	        (state.app_usize as *mut NmpApp, pk, rev)
    69	    };
    70	
    71	    // Reentrant dispatch: open_author from inside the listener callback.
    72	    // nmp_app_open_author enqueues to actor's mpsc Sender (Send+Sync).
    73	    // This is fire-and-forget per bible #3 — the send must not block.
    74	    nmp_app_open_author(app_ptr, pk_cstr.as_ptr());
    75	    REENTRANT_DISPATCHES.fetch_add(1, Ordering::Relaxed);
    76	
    77	    let total_ns = t0.elapsed().as_nanos() as u64;
    78	
    79	    // Watchdog: flag deadlock if callback took too long.
    80	    if total_ns > DEADLOCK_THRESHOLD_MS * 1_000_000 {
    81	        DEADLOCKS.fetch_add(1, Ordering::Relaxed);
    82	        WATCHDOG_TRIPPED.store(true, Ordering::Release);
    83	    }
    84	
    85	    if let Ok(mut state) = unsafe { (*state_ptr).lock() } {
    86	        state.cb_latencies_ns.push(total_ns);
    87	        state.revs.push(rev);
    88	    }
    89	}
    90	
    91	fn extract_rev(bytes: &[u8]) -> Option<u64> {
    92	    let s = std::str::from_utf8(bytes).ok()?;
    93	    let key = "\"rev\":";
    94	    let pos = s.find(key)?;
    95	    let rest = &s[pos + key.len()..];
    96	    let end = rest.find([',', '}', ' ', '\n']).unwrap_or(rest.len());
    97	    rest[..end].trim().parse::<u64>().ok()
    98	}
    99	
   100	pub(crate) struct S5Config {
   101	    /// Total run duration (spec: 30 s).
   102	    pub(crate) duration: Duration,
   103	    /// Events/sec driving configure() triggers.
   104	    pub(crate) events_per_sec: u32,
   105	}
   106	
   107	impl Default for S5Config {
   108	    fn default() -> Self {
   109	        S5Config {
   110	            duration: Duration::from_secs(30),
   111	            events_per_sec: 50,
   112	        }
   113	    }
   114	}
   115	
   116	pub(crate) fn run(cfg: S5Config, report: &mut ScenarioMetrics) {
   117	    let wall_start = Instant::now();
   118	    DEADLOCKS.store(0, Ordering::Relaxed);
   119	    EMIT_COUNT.store(0, Ordering::Relaxed);
   120	    REENTRANT_DISPATCHES.store(0, Ordering::Relaxed);
   121	    WATCHDOG_TRIPPED.store(false, Ordering::Release);
   122	
   123	    let app: *mut NmpApp = nmp_app_new();
   124	
   125	    // Build callback state with 10 pubkeys to rotate across.
   126	    let pubkeys = test_pubkeys(10);
   127	    let state = Box::new(Mutex::new(ReentryState {
   128	        app_usize: app as usize,
   129	        pubkeys,
   130	        cb_latencies_ns: Vec::new(),
   131	        revs: Vec::new(),
   132	    }));
   133	    let ctx = Box::into_raw(state) as *mut c_void;
   134	
   135	    nmp_app_set_update_callback(app, ctx, Some(reentrant_cb));
   136	    // Configure-not-Start: S5 tests reentrancy safety, not relay connectivity.
   137	    nmp_app_configure(app, 0, 80, 4);
   138	
   139	    // Open firehose to drive ingest events.
   140	    let tag = std::ffi::CString::new("reentrancy-test").expect("no nuls");
   141	    nmp_app_open_firehose_tag(app, tag.as_ptr());
   142	
   143	    // Drive configure() at events_per_sec rate to trigger emits.
   144	    let interval = Duration::from_nanos(1_000_000_000 / cfg.events_per_sec.max(1) as u64);
   145	    let mut next_tick = Instant::now();
   146	
   147	    while wall_start.elapsed() < cfg.duration {
   148	        nmp_app_configure(app, 0, 80, 4);
   149	        next_tick += interval;
   150	        if let Some(sleep) = next_tick.checked_duration_since(Instant::now()) {
   151	            std::thread::sleep(sleep);
   152	        }
   153	    }
   154	
   155	    // Grace period for reentrant dispatches to complete.
   156	    std::thread::sleep(Duration::from_millis(500));
   157	
   158	    let wall_elapsed = wall_start.elapsed().as_secs_f64();
   159	
   160	    nmp_app_set_update_callback(app, std::ptr::null_mut(), None);
   161	    nmp_app_free(app);
   162	
   163	    // SAFETY: callback is cleared; no other references to ctx remain.
   164	    let state = unsafe { Box::from_raw(ctx as *mut Mutex<ReentryState>) };
   165	    let state = state.lock().unwrap();
   166	
   167	    let emit_count = EMIT_COUNT.load(Ordering::Relaxed);
   168	    let reentrant_count = REENTRANT_DISPATCHES.load(Ordering::Relaxed);
   169	    let deadlock_count = DEADLOCKS.load(Ordering::Relaxed);
   170	
   171	    // Listener CPU per emit (avg).
   172	    let avg_cb_ns = if state.cb_latencies_ns.is_empty() {
   173	        0.0
   174	    } else {
   175	        state.cb_latencies_ns.iter().sum::<u64>() as f64 / state.cb_latencies_ns.len() as f64
   176	    };
   177	    let avg_cb_ms = avg_cb_ns / 1_000_000.0;
   178	
   179	    // Rev monotonicity.
   180	    let revs_monotonic = is_strictly_increasing_nonzero(&state.revs);
   181	
   182	    // G-S5 gates.
   183	    report.gates.push(
   184	        Gate::eq("deadlocks", deadlock_count as f64, 0.0)
   185	            .with_note("G-S5: zero deadlocks (5 s watchdog per emit)"),
   186	    );
   187	    report.gates.push(
   188	        Gate::gte("reentrant_dispatches", reentrant_count as f64, 100.0)
   189	            .with_note("G-S5: dispatch-from-callback emits processed >= 100 over 30 s"),
   190	    );
   191	    report.gates.push(
   192	        Gate::eq("rev_monotonic", if revs_monotonic { 1.0 } else { 0.0 }, 1.0)
   193	            .with_note("G-S5: out-of-order callback->dispatch pairs == 0 (rev monotonic)"),
   194	    );
   195	    report.gates.push(
   196	        Gate::lte("avg_cb_ms", avg_cb_ms, 2.0)
   197	            .with_note("G-S5: listener thread CPU per emit avg <= 2 ms"),
   198	    );
   199	    // Dispatch loss: every emit should have produced one reentrant dispatch.
   200	    let dispatch_loss = emit_count.saturating_sub(reentrant_count);
   201	    report.gates.push(
   202	        Gate::eq("dispatch_loss", dispatch_loss as f64, 0.0)
   203	            .with_note("G-S5: dispatch loss == 0"),
   204	    );
   205	
   206	    report.notes.push(format!(
   207	        "Emits: {emit_count}; reentrant dispatches: {reentrant_count}; \
   208	         deadlocks: {deadlock_count}; avg callback: {avg_cb_ms:.3} ms"
   209	    ));
   210	    report.notes.push(
   211	        "Reentrant dispatch is fire-and-forget (bible #3): nmp_app_open_author enqueues \
   212	         to actor mpsc channel; does not block listener thread or re-lock any mutex."
   213	            .to_string(),
   214	    );
   215	
   216	    report.measurements = json!({
   217	        "emit_count": emit_count,
   218	        "reentrant_dispatches": reentrant_count,
   219	        "deadlocks": deadlock_count,
   220	        "dispatch_loss": dispatch_loss,
   221	        "avg_cb_ms": avg_cb_ms,
   222	        "rev_monotonic": revs_monotonic,
   223	        "wall_seconds": wall_elapsed,
   224	        "watchdog_tripped": WATCHDOG_TRIPPED.load(Ordering::Relaxed),
   225	    });
   226	
   227	    report.finish(wall_elapsed);
   228	}
   229	
   230	fn is_strictly_increasing_nonzero(revs: &[u64]) -> bool {
   231	    let non_zero: Vec<u64> = revs.iter().copied().filter(|&r| r > 0).collect();
   232	    if non_zero.len() < 2 {
   233	        return true;
   234	    }
   235	    non_zero.windows(2).all(|w| w[1] > w[0])
   236	}

 succeeded in 0ms:
     1	//! Counting allocator — vendored from reactivity-bench (ADR-0004).
     2	//!
     3	//! Installed as `#[global_allocator]` in this binary only. Provides
     4	//! snapshot-based heap accounting used by S1, S2, S3, S5 to detect
     5	//! per-event allocation growth without Instruments.
     6	//!
     7	//! D8: zero per-event allocations after warmup is the primary invariant
     8	//! this allocator validates.
     9	
    10	use std::alloc::{GlobalAlloc, Layout, System};
    11	use std::sync::atomic::{AtomicU64, AtomicUsize, Ordering};
    12	
    13	#[global_allocator]
    14	pub(crate) static GLOBAL: CountingAllocator = CountingAllocator;
    15	
    16	pub(crate) struct CountingAllocator;
    17	
    18	static TOTAL_ALLOCATIONS: AtomicU64 = AtomicU64::new(0);
    19	static TOTAL_ALLOCATED_BYTES: AtomicU64 = AtomicU64::new(0);
    20	static CURRENT_HEAP_BYTES: AtomicUsize = AtomicUsize::new(0);
    21	static PEAK_HEAP_BYTES: AtomicUsize = AtomicUsize::new(0);
    22	
    23	unsafe impl GlobalAlloc for CountingAllocator {
    24	    unsafe fn alloc(&self, layout: Layout) -> *mut u8 {
    25	        let ptr = System.alloc(layout);
    26	        if !ptr.is_null() {
    27	            record_alloc(layout.size());
    28	        }
    29	        ptr
    30	    }
    31	
    32	    unsafe fn dealloc(&self, ptr: *mut u8, layout: Layout) {
    33	        System.dealloc(ptr, layout);
    34	        CURRENT_HEAP_BYTES.fetch_sub(layout.size(), Ordering::Relaxed);
    35	    }
    36	
    37	    unsafe fn realloc(&self, ptr: *mut u8, layout: Layout, new_size: usize) -> *mut u8 {
    38	        let ptr = System.realloc(ptr, layout, new_size);
    39	        if !ptr.is_null() {
    40	            TOTAL_ALLOCATIONS.fetch_add(1, Ordering::Relaxed);
    41	            TOTAL_ALLOCATED_BYTES.fetch_add(new_size as u64, Ordering::Relaxed);
    42	            if new_size >= layout.size() {
    43	                update_current_heap(new_size - layout.size());
    44	            } else {
    45	                CURRENT_HEAP_BYTES.fetch_sub(layout.size() - new_size, Ordering::Relaxed);
    46	            }
    47	        }
    48	        ptr
    49	    }
    50	}
    51	
    52	fn record_alloc(size: usize) {
    53	    TOTAL_ALLOCATIONS.fetch_add(1, Ordering::Relaxed);
    54	    TOTAL_ALLOCATED_BYTES.fetch_add(size as u64, Ordering::Relaxed);
    55	    update_current_heap(size);
    56	}
    57	
    58	fn update_current_heap(additional: usize) {
    59	    let current = CURRENT_HEAP_BYTES.fetch_add(additional, Ordering::Relaxed) + additional;
    60	    let mut peak = PEAK_HEAP_BYTES.load(Ordering::Relaxed);
    61	    while current > peak {
    62	        match PEAK_HEAP_BYTES.compare_exchange_weak(
    63	            peak,
    64	            current,
    65	            Ordering::Relaxed,
    66	            Ordering::Relaxed,
    67	        ) {
    68	            Ok(_) => break,
    69	            Err(observed) => peak = observed,
    70	        }
    71	    }
    72	}
    73	
    74	/// Point-in-time snapshot of heap counters. Take two snapshots and diff
    75	/// them to measure allocation growth across a bounded window (e.g., one
    76	/// emit cycle after warmup).
    77	///
    78	/// The `current_heap_bytes` field tracks NET live heap (allocations - frees),
    79	/// which is the correct metric for the D8 "zero per-event allocations" gate:
    80	/// transient per-cycle allocations (e.g., CString) that are immediately freed
    81	/// do not cause NET heap growth and thus pass the gate.
    82	#[derive(Clone, Copy, Debug)]
    83	pub(crate) struct AllocSnapshot {
    84	    pub(crate) allocations: u64,
    85	    pub(crate) allocated_bytes: u64,
    86	    /// Net live heap bytes at snapshot time. This is what D8 gates against.
    87	    pub(crate) current_heap_bytes: usize,
    88	}
    89	
    90	pub(crate) fn alloc_snapshot() -> AllocSnapshot {
    91	    AllocSnapshot {
    92	        allocations: TOTAL_ALLOCATIONS.load(Ordering::Relaxed),
    93	        allocated_bytes: TOTAL_ALLOCATED_BYTES.load(Ordering::Relaxed),
    94	        current_heap_bytes: CURRENT_HEAP_BYTES.load(Ordering::Relaxed),
    95	    }
    96	}
    97	
    98	impl AllocSnapshot {
    99	    /// Allocations added since `earlier`.
   100	    pub(crate) fn allocs_since(&self, earlier: &AllocSnapshot) -> u64 {
   101	        self.allocations.saturating_sub(earlier.allocations)
   102	    }
   103	
   104	    /// Bytes allocated (total gross, not net) since `earlier`.
   105	    pub(crate) fn bytes_since(&self, earlier: &AllocSnapshot) -> u64 {
   106	        self.allocated_bytes.saturating_sub(earlier.allocated_bytes)
   107	    }
   108	
   109	    /// Net live-heap delta since `earlier`.
   110	    ///
   111	    /// A negative result (earlier had more live bytes) means the allocator
   112	    /// reclaimed memory. Zero or negative is the D8 passing condition.
   113	    pub(crate) fn net_heap_delta(&self, earlier: &AllocSnapshot) -> i64 {
   114	        self.current_heap_bytes as i64 - earlier.current_heap_bytes as i64
   115	    }
   116	}

     1	//! ffi-stress — FFI hardening stress harness (M10.5 phase 1).
     2	//!
     3	//! Drives the `nmp_app_*` C symbols via extern declarations (same ABI Swift
     4	//! uses) and verifies S1–S5 gate conditions from
     5	//! `docs/design/ffi-hardening/gates.md`.
     6	//!
     7	//! Usage:
     8	//!   ffi-stress <scenario> [--duration <D>] [--threads <N>] [--fail-on-gate]
     9	//!                         [--write-report]
    10	//!
    11	//! Scenarios: mount-unmount (S1) | dispatch-flood (S2) | snapshot-pressure (S3)
    12	//!            | reconciler-backpressure (S4) | reentrancy (S5)
    13	//!
    14	//! See `docs/design/ffi-hardening/harness.md` §1.2 for the full CLI reference.
    15	
    16	mod allocator;
    17	mod ffi;
    18	mod gate;
    19	mod report;
    20	mod s1_mount_unmount;
    21	mod s2_dispatch_flood;
    22	mod s3_snapshot_pressure;
    23	mod s4_reconciler_backpressure;
    24	mod s5_reentrancy;
    25	
    26	use report::{now_unix_seconds, write_scenario_report, ScenarioMetrics};
    27	use std::process;
    28	use std::time::Duration;
    29	
    30	fn main() {
    31	    let args: Vec<String> = std::env::args().collect();
    32	    let cli = match Cli::parse(&args[1..]) {
    33	        Ok(cli) => cli,
    34	        Err(msg) => {
    35	            eprintln!("ffi-stress: {msg}");
    36	            eprintln!("{USAGE}");
    37	            process::exit(1);
    38	        }
    39	    };
    40	
    41	    let mut overall_pass = true;
    42	
    43	    for scenario in &cli.scenarios {
    44	        let mut metrics = ScenarioMetrics::new(scenario_name(scenario));
    45	        metrics.started_at_unix = now_unix_seconds();
    46	
    47	        eprintln!("ffi-stress: running {} …", scenario_name(scenario));
    48	
    49	        match scenario {
    50	            Scenario::MountUnmount => {
    51	                let cfg = s1_mount_unmount::S1Config {
    52	                    duration: cli.duration,
    53	                    ..Default::default()
    54	                };
    55	                s1_mount_unmount::run(cfg, &mut metrics);
    56	            }
    57	            Scenario::DispatchFlood => {
    58	                let cfg = s2_dispatch_flood::S2Config {
    59	                    duration: cli.duration,
    60	                    threads: cli.threads,
    61	                    ..Default::default()
    62	                };
    63	                s2_dispatch_flood::run(cfg, &mut metrics);
    64	            }
    65	            Scenario::SnapshotPressure => {
    66	                let cfg = s3_snapshot_pressure::S3Config {
    67	                    warmup_duration: (cli.duration / 3).min(Duration::from_secs(10)),
    68	                    ..Default::default()
    69	                };
    70	                s3_snapshot_pressure::run(cfg, &mut metrics);
    71	            }
    72	            Scenario::ReconcilerBackpressure => {
    73	                let cfg = s4_reconciler_backpressure::S4Config {
    74	                    duration: cli.duration,
    75	                    ..Default::default()
    76	                };
    77	                s4_reconciler_backpressure::run(cfg, &mut metrics);
    78	            }
    79	            Scenario::Reentrancy => {
    80	                let cfg = s5_reentrancy::S5Config {
    81	                    duration: cli.duration,
    82	                    ..Default::default()
    83	                };
    84	                s5_reentrancy::run(cfg, &mut metrics);
    85	            }
    86	        }
    87	
    88	        let passed = metrics.passed;
    89	        overall_pass &= passed;
    90	
    91	        eprintln!(
    92	            "ffi-stress: {} — {} ({}/{} gates)",
    93	            scenario_name(scenario),
    94	            if passed { "PASS" } else { "FAIL" },
    95	            metrics.gates.iter().filter(|g| g.passed).count(),
    96	            metrics.gates.len()
    97	        );
    98	        for gate in &metrics.gates {
    99	            let sym = if gate.passed { "  ok" } else { "FAIL" };
   100	            eprintln!("  [{sym}] {}: {:.4} (op={:?} threshold={:.4})",
   101	                gate.name, gate.measured, gate.op, gate.threshold);
   102	        }
   103	
   104	        if cli.write_report {
   105	            if let Err(e) = write_scenario_report(&metrics) {
   106	                eprintln!("ffi-stress: failed to write report: {e}");
   107	            }
   108	        }
   109	
   110	        // Print JSON to stdout for pipeline consumption.
   111	        println!(
   112	            "{}",
   113	            serde_json::to_string_pretty(&metrics).expect("serialize")
   114	        );
   115	    }
   116	
   117	    if cli.fail_on_gate && !overall_pass {
   118	        process::exit(2);
   119	    }
   120	}
   121	
   122	// ---- CLI parsing -----------------------------------------------------------
   123	
   124	const USAGE: &str = r#"Usage: ffi-stress <scenario> [options]
   125	
   126	Scenarios:
   127	  mount-unmount            S1 — view-handle wrapper refcount churn
   128	  dispatch-flood           S2 — mpsc backpressure (10k/s, 4 threads)
   129	  snapshot-pressure        S3 — AppUpdate::FullState serialization pressure
   130	  reconciler-backpressure  S4 — 250 ms main-thread stall simulation
   131	  reentrancy               S5 — dispatch from inside reconciler callback
   132	
   133	Options:
   134	  --duration <D>           Wall-clock duration (e.g. 60s, 10m). Default: scenario-specific.
   135	  --threads <N>            Caller thread count (S2 default: 4).
   136	  --fail-on-gate           Exit 2 if any gate fails.
   137	  --write-report           Write docs/perf/m10.5/<scenario>/{metrics.json,report.md}.
   138	"#;
   139	
   140	#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy)]
   141	enum Scenario {
   142	    MountUnmount,
   143	    DispatchFlood,
   144	    SnapshotPressure,
   145	    ReconcilerBackpressure,
   146	    Reentrancy,
   147	}
   148	
   149	fn scenario_name(s: &Scenario) -> &'static str {
   150	    match s {
   151	        Scenario::MountUnmount => "S1-mount-unmount",
   152	        Scenario::DispatchFlood => "S2-dispatch-flood",
   153	        Scenario::SnapshotPressure => "S3-snapshot-pressure",
   154	        Scenario::ReconcilerBackpressure => "S4-reconciler-backpressure",
   155	        Scenario::Reentrancy => "S5-reentrancy",
   156	    }
   157	}
   158	
   159	struct Cli {
   160	    scenarios: Vec<Scenario>,
   161	    duration: Duration,
   162	    threads: usize,
   163	    fail_on_gate: bool,
   164	    write_report: bool,
   165	}
   166	
   167	impl Cli {
   168	    fn parse(args: &[String]) -> Result<Cli, String> {
   169	        let mut scenarios: Vec<Scenario> = Vec::new();
   170	        let mut duration: Option<Duration> = None;
   171	        let mut threads: usize = 4;
   172	        let mut fail_on_gate = false;
   173	        let mut write_report = false;
   174	
   175	        let mut i = 0;
   176	        while i < args.len() {
   177	            match args[i].as_str() {
   178	                "--duration" => {
   179	                    i += 1;
   180	                    let s = args.get(i).ok_or("--duration requires a value")?;
   181	                    duration = Some(parse_duration(s)?);
   182	                }
   183	                "--threads" => {
   184	                    i += 1;
   185	                    let s = args.get(i).ok_or("--threads requires a value")?;
   186	                    threads = s.parse::<usize>().map_err(|_| "invalid --threads value")?;
   187	                }
   188	                "--fail-on-gate" => fail_on_gate = true,
   189	                "--write-report" => write_report = true,
   190	                s if !s.starts_with('-') => {
   191	                    let sc = parse_scenario(s)?;
   192	                    scenarios.push(sc);
   193	                }
   194	                unknown => return Err(format!("unknown argument: {unknown}")),
   195	            }
   196	            i += 1;
   197	        }
   198	
   199	        if scenarios.is_empty() {
   200	            return Err("no scenario specified".to_string());
   201	        }
   202	
   203	        // Default durations per scenario (fast mode).
   204	        let duration = duration.unwrap_or_else(|| match scenarios.first() {
   205	            Some(Scenario::MountUnmount) => Duration::from_secs(60),
   206	            Some(Scenario::DispatchFlood) => Duration::from_secs(30),
   207	            Some(Scenario::SnapshotPressure) => Duration::from_secs(30),
   208	            Some(Scenario::ReconcilerBackpressure) => Duration::from_secs(60),
   209	            Some(Scenario::Reentrancy) => Duration::from_secs(30),
   210	            None => Duration::from_secs(30),
   211	        });
   212	
   213	        Ok(Cli {
   214	            scenarios,
   215	            duration,
   216	            threads,
   217	            fail_on_gate,
   218	            write_report,
   219	        })
   220	    }
   221	}
   222	
   223	fn parse_scenario(s: &str) -> Result<Scenario, String> {
   224	    match s {
   225	        "mount-unmount" | "s1" => Ok(Scenario::MountUnmount),
   226	        "dispatch-flood" | "s2" => Ok(Scenario::DispatchFlood),
   227	        "snapshot-pressure" | "s3" => Ok(Scenario::SnapshotPressure),
   228	        "reconciler-backpressure" | "s4" => Ok(Scenario::ReconcilerBackpressure),
   229	        "reentrancy" | "s5" => Ok(Scenario::Reentrancy),
   230	        other => Err(format!("unknown scenario: {other}")),
   231	    }
   232	}
   233	
   234	fn parse_duration(s: &str) -> Result<Duration, String> {
   235	    if let Some(mins) = s.strip_suffix('m') {
   236	        let m: u64 = mins
   237	            .parse()
   238	            .map_err(|_| format!("invalid duration: {s}"))?;
   239	        return Ok(Duration::from_secs(m * 60));
   240	    }
   241	    if let Some(secs) = s.strip_suffix('s') {
   242	        let s: u64 = secs
   243	            .parse()
   244	            .map_err(|_| format!("invalid duration: {s}"))?;
   245	        return Ok(Duration::from_secs(s));
   246	    }
   247	    // Plain number = seconds.
   248	    s.parse::<u64>()
   249	        .map(Duration::from_secs)
   250	        .map_err(|_| format!("invalid duration: {s}"))
   251	}

 succeeded in 0ms:
     1	//! Thin re-exports of the 14 `nmp_app_*` entry-points from `nmp-core`.
     2	//!
     3	//! The harness calls these directly via the Rust rlib dependency (the
     4	//! alternative path from `harness.md` §1.6). The functions take `*mut NmpApp`
     5	//! in their Rust signatures; Swift sees them as `*mut c_void` via the C ABI.
     6	//! Both paths exercise identical FFI-surface code paths.
     7	//!
     8	//! The symbols remain `#[no_mangle] pub extern "C"` on the nmp-core side so
     9	//! they are still reachable from Swift/C unchanged.
    10	
    11	pub(crate) use nmp_core::{
    12	    nmp_app_claim_profile, nmp_app_close_author, nmp_app_configure, nmp_app_free, nmp_app_new,
    13	    nmp_app_open_author, nmp_app_open_firehose_tag, nmp_app_release_profile,
    14	    nmp_app_set_update_callback, NmpApp,
    15	};
    16	
    17	/// Generate N deterministic lowercase 64-hex-char pubkeys suitable for all
    18	/// FFI calls that require `is_hex_pubkey` validation to pass.
    19	pub(crate) fn test_pubkeys(count: usize) -> Vec<std::ffi::CString> {
    20	    (0..count)
    21	        .map(|i| {
    22	            // 64 hex chars derived from index — valid by construction.
    23	            let hex = format!("{:0>16x}{:0>16x}{:0>16x}{:0>16x}", i, i + 1, i + 2, i + 3);
    24	            std::ffi::CString::new(hex).expect("no interior nuls in hex string")
    25	        })
    26	        .collect()
    27	}
    28	
    29	/// Read current process RSS in bytes.
    30	/// On macOS uses `task_info(MACH_TASK_BASIC_INFO)`. Returns 0 elsewhere.
    31	pub(crate) fn process_rss_bytes() -> u64 {
    32	    #[cfg(target_os = "macos")]
    33	    {
    34	        use std::mem;
    35	
    36	        #[repr(C)]
    37	        #[derive(Default)]
    38	        struct MachTaskBasicInfo {
    39	            virtual_size: u64,
    40	            resident_size: u64,
    41	            resident_size_max: u64,
    42	            user_time_seconds: u32,
    43	            user_time_microseconds: u32,
    44	            system_time_seconds: u32,
    45	            system_time_microseconds: u32,
    46	            policy: i32,
    47	            suspend_count: i32,
    48	        }
    49	
    50	        extern "C" {
    51	            fn task_self_trap() -> u32;
    52	            fn task_info(
    53	                target_task: u32,
    54	                flavor: u32,
    55	                task_info_out: *mut u32,
    56	                task_info_out_cnt: *mut u32,
    57	            ) -> i32;
    58	        }
    59	
    60	        const MACH_TASK_BASIC_INFO: u32 = 20;
    61	        let mut info = MachTaskBasicInfo::default();
    62	        let mut count = (mem::size_of::<MachTaskBasicInfo>() / mem::size_of::<u32>()) as u32;
    63	        let ret = unsafe {
    64	            task_info(
    65	                task_self_trap(),
    66	                MACH_TASK_BASIC_INFO,
    67	                &mut info as *mut MachTaskBasicInfo as *mut u32,
    68	                &mut count,
    69	            )
    70	        };
    71	        if ret == 0 { info.resident_size } else { 0 }
    72	    }
    73	    #[cfg(not(target_os = "macos"))]
    74	    {
    75	        0
    76	    }
    77	}

codex
The docs are stricter than the harness in several places: some implemented checks are proxies or phase-2 notes rather than the required gates. I’m doing one compile pass now to separate spec mismatches from build breakage.

 succeeded in 0ms:
     1	use crate::actor::{run_actor, ActorCommand};
     2	use crate::kernel::{is_hex_id, is_hex_pubkey};
     3	use crate::relay::{DEFAULT_EMIT_HZ, DEFAULT_VISIBLE_LIMIT};
     4	use std::ffi::{c_char, c_uint, c_void, CStr, CString};
     5	use std::sync::mpsc::{self, Sender};
     6	use std::sync::{Arc, Mutex};
     7	use std::thread;
     8	use std::thread::JoinHandle;
     9	
    10	type UpdateCallback = extern "C" fn(*mut c_void, *const c_char);
    11	
    12	#[derive(Clone, Copy)]
    13	struct UpdateCallbackRegistration {
    14	    context: usize,
    15	    callback: UpdateCallback,
    16	}
    17	
    18	pub struct NmpApp {
    19	    tx: Sender<ActorCommand>,
    20	    update_callback: Arc<Mutex<Option<UpdateCallbackRegistration>>>,
    21	    actor: Mutex<Option<JoinHandle<()>>>,
    22	    update_listener: Mutex<Option<JoinHandle<()>>>,
    23	}
    24	
    25	impl Drop for NmpApp {
    26	    fn drop(&mut self) {
    27	        if let Ok(mut callback) = self.update_callback.lock() {
    28	            *callback = None;
    29	        }
    30	        let _ = self.tx.send(ActorCommand::Shutdown);
    31	        if let Ok(mut actor) = self.actor.lock() {
    32	            if let Some(handle) = actor.take() {
    33	                let _ = handle.join();
    34	            }
    35	        }
    36	        if let Ok(mut listener) = self.update_listener.lock() {
    37	            if let Some(handle) = listener.take() {
    38	                let _ = handle.join();
    39	            }
    40	        }
    41	    }
    42	}
    43	
    44	#[no_mangle]
    45	pub extern "C" fn nmp_app_new() -> *mut NmpApp {
    46	    let (command_tx, command_rx) = mpsc::channel();
    47	    let (update_tx, update_rx) = mpsc::channel();
    48	    let update_callback: Arc<Mutex<Option<UpdateCallbackRegistration>>> =
    49	        Arc::new(Mutex::new(None));
    50	    let listener_callback = Arc::clone(&update_callback);
    51	    let actor = thread::spawn(move || run_actor(command_rx, update_tx));
    52	    let update_listener = thread::spawn(move || {
    53	        while let Ok(update) = update_rx.recv() {
    54	            let Ok(payload) = CString::new(update) else {
    55	                continue;
    56	            };
    57	            let callback = listener_callback.lock().ok().and_then(|guard| *guard);
    58	            if let Some(registration) = callback {
    59	                (registration.callback)(registration.context as *mut c_void, payload.as_ptr());
    60	            }
    61	        }
    62	    });
    63	
    64	    Box::into_raw(Box::new(NmpApp {
    65	        tx: command_tx,
    66	        update_callback,
    67	        actor: Mutex::new(Some(actor)),
    68	        update_listener: Mutex::new(Some(update_listener)),
    69	    }))
    70	}
    71	
    72	// SAFETY: `app` is a raw pointer from `nmp_app_new()`. The function is `extern "C"` (callable
    73	// from Swift/C) so it cannot be marked `unsafe` at the Rust level; the caller guarantees the
    74	// pointer contract. The `allow` suppresses the clippy::not_unsafe_ptr_arg_deref lint which
    75	// does not distinguish between `extern "C"` FFI boundaries and ordinary Rust functions.
    76	#[allow(clippy::not_unsafe_ptr_arg_deref)]
    77	#[no_mangle]
    78	pub extern "C" fn nmp_app_free(app: *mut NmpApp) {
    79	    if !app.is_null() {
    80	        // SAFETY: caller guarantees app is a valid pointer allocated by nmp_app_new().
    81	        unsafe {
    82	            drop(Box::from_raw(app));
    83	        }
    84	    }
    85	}
    86	
    87	#[no_mangle]
    88	pub extern "C" fn nmp_app_set_update_callback(
    89	    app: *mut NmpApp,
    90	    context: *mut c_void,
    91	    callback: Option<UpdateCallback>,
    92	) {
    93	    let Some(app) = app_ref(app) else {
    94	        return;
    95	    };
    96	    let Ok(mut slot) = app.update_callback.lock() else {
    97	        return;
    98	    };
    99	    *slot = callback.map(|callback| UpdateCallbackRegistration {
   100	        context: context as usize,
   101	        callback,
   102	    });
   103	}
   104	
   105	#[no_mangle]
   106	pub extern "C" fn nmp_app_start(
   107	    app: *mut NmpApp,
   108	    _events_per_second: c_uint,
   109	    visible_limit: c_uint,
   110	    emit_hz: c_uint,
   111	) {
   112	    let Some(app) = app_ref(app) else {
   113	        return;
   114	    };
   115	
   116	    let _ = app.tx.send(ActorCommand::Start {
   117	        visible_limit: clamp_visible(visible_limit),
   118	        emit_hz: clamp_emit_hz(emit_hz),
   119	    });
   120	}
   121	
   122	#[no_mangle]
   123	pub extern "C" fn nmp_app_configure(
   124	    app: *mut NmpApp,
   125	    _events_per_second: c_uint,
   126	    visible_limit: c_uint,
   127	    emit_hz: c_uint,
   128	) {
   129	    let Some(app) = app_ref(app) else {
   130	        return;
   131	    };
   132	
   133	    let _ = app.tx.send(ActorCommand::Configure {
   134	        visible_limit: clamp_visible(visible_limit),
   135	        emit_hz: clamp_emit_hz(emit_hz),
   136	    });
   137	}
   138	
   139	#[no_mangle]
   140	pub extern "C" fn nmp_app_stop(app: *mut NmpApp) {
   141	    let Some(app) = app_ref(app) else {
   142	        return;
   143	    };
   144	    let _ = app.tx.send(ActorCommand::Stop);
   145	}
   146	
   147	#[no_mangle]
   148	pub extern "C" fn nmp_app_reset(app: *mut NmpApp) {
   149	    let Some(app) = app_ref(app) else {
   150	        return;
   151	    };
   152	    let _ = app.tx.send(ActorCommand::Reset);
   153	}
   154	
   155	#[no_mangle]
   156	pub extern "C" fn nmp_app_open_author(app: *mut NmpApp, pubkey: *const c_char) {
   157	    let Some(app) = app_ref(app) else {
   158	        return;
   159	    };
   160	    let Some(pubkey) = c_string_argument(pubkey) else {
   161	        return;
   162	    };
   163	    if !is_hex_pubkey(&pubkey) {
   164	        return;
   165	    }
   166	
   167	    let _ = app.tx.send(ActorCommand::OpenAuthor { pubkey });
   168	}
   169

   171	pub extern "C" fn nmp_app_open_thread(app: *mut NmpApp, event_id: *const c_char) {
   172	    let Some(app) = app_ref(app) else {
   173	        return;
   174	    };
   175	    let Some(event_id) = c_string_argument(event_id) else {
   176	        return;
   177	    };
   178	    if !is_hex_id(&event_id) {
   179	        return;
   180	    }
   181	
   182	    let _ = app.tx.send(ActorCommand::OpenThread { event_id });
   183	}
   184

   186	pub extern "C" fn nmp_app_open_firehose_tag(app: *mut NmpApp, tag: *const c_char) {
   187	    let Some(app) = app_ref(app) else {
   188	        return;
   189	    };

   191	        return;
   192	    };
   193	
   194	    let _ = app.tx.send(ActorCommand::OpenFirehoseTag { tag });
   195	}
   196	
   197	#[no_mangle]
   198	pub extern "C" fn nmp_app_claim_profile(
   199	    app: *mut NmpApp,
   200	    pubkey: *const c_char,
   201	    consumer_id: *const c_char,
   202	) {
   203	    let Some(app) = app_ref(app) else {
   204	        return;
   205	    };
   206	    let Some(pubkey) = c_string_argument(pubkey) else {
   207	        return;
   208	    };
   209	    let Some(consumer_id) = c_string_argument(consumer_id) else {
   210	        return;
   211	    };
   212	    if !is_hex_pubkey(&pubkey) {
   213	        return;
   214	    }
   215	
   216	    let _ = app.tx.send(ActorCommand::ClaimProfile {
   217	        pubkey,
   218	        consumer_id,
   219	    });
   220	}
   221	
   222	#[no_mangle]
   223	pub extern "C" fn nmp_app_release_profile(
   224	    app: *mut NmpApp,
   225	    pubkey: *const c_char,
   226	    consumer_id: *const c_char,
   227	) {
   228	    let Some(app) = app_ref(app) else {
   229	        return;
   230	    };
   231	    let Some(pubkey) = c_string_argument(pubkey) else {
   232	        return;
   233	    };
   234	    let Some(consumer_id) = c_string_argument(consumer_id) else {
   235	        return;
   236	    };
   237	    if !is_hex_pubkey(&pubkey) {
   238	        return;
   239	    }
   240	
   241	    let _ = app.tx.send(ActorCommand::ReleaseProfile {
   242	        pubkey,
   243	        consumer_id,
   244	    });
   245	}
   246	
   247	#[no_mangle]
   248	pub extern "C" fn nmp_app_close_author(app: *mut NmpApp, pubkey: *const c_char) {
   249	    let Some(app) = app_ref(app) else {
   250	        return;
   251	    };
   252	    let Some(pubkey) = c_string_argument(pubkey) else {
   253	        return;
   254	    };
   255	    if !is_hex_pubkey(&pubkey) {
   256	        return;
   257	    }
   258	
   259	    let _ = app.tx.send(ActorCommand::CloseAuthor { pubkey });
   260	}
   261	
   262	#[no_mangle]
   263	pub extern "C" fn nmp_app_close_thread(app: *mut NmpApp, event_id: *const c_char) {
   264	    let Some(app) = app_ref(app) else {
   265	        return;
   266	    };
   267	    let Some(event_id) = c_string_argument(event_id) else {
   268	        return;
   269	    };
   270	    if !is_hex_id(&event_id) {
   271	        return;
   272	    }
   273	
   274	    let _ = app.tx.send(ActorCommand::CloseThread { event_id });
   275	}
   276	
   277	fn app_ref<'a>(app: *mut NmpApp) -> Option<&'a NmpApp> {
   278	    if app.is_null() {
   279	        None
   280	    } else {
   281	        // SAFETY: caller guarantees non-null app is a valid NmpApp pointer.
   282	        Some(unsafe { &*app })
   283	    }
   284	}
   285	
   286	fn c_string_argument(ptr: *const c_char) -> Option<String> {
   287	    if ptr.is_null() {
   288	        return None;
   289	    }
   290	
   291	    // SAFETY: caller guarantees ptr is a valid null-terminated C string.
   292	    // Validation: to_str() will reject invalid UTF-8.
   293	    unsafe { CStr::from_ptr(ptr) }
   294	        .to_str()
   295	        .ok()
   296	        .map(str::trim)
   297	        .filter(|value| !value.is_empty())
   298	        .map(ToOwned::to_owned)
   299	}
   300	
   301	fn clamp_visible(visible_limit: c_uint) -> usize {
   302	    if visible_limit == 0 {
   303	        DEFAULT_VISIBLE_LIMIT
   304	    } else {
   305	        visible_limit.clamp(1, 500) as usize
   306	    }
   307	}
   308	
   309	fn clamp_emit_hz(emit_hz: c_uint) -> u32 {
   310	    if emit_hz == 0 {
   311	        DEFAULT_EMIT_HZ
   312	    } else {
   313	        emit_hz.clamp(1, 12)
   314	    }
   315	}

 succeeded in 0ms:
     1	//! Actor main loop — message routing, command dispatch, relay event handling.
     2	//!
     3	//! Idle-tick timing helpers are in `tick.rs`.
     4	//! Relay lifecycle helpers are in `relay_mgmt.rs`.
     5	
     6	mod relay_mgmt;
     7	mod tick;
     8	
     9	use relay_mgmt::{
    10	    all_relays_connected, bridge_commands, bridge_relays, close_relays, maybe_send_startup,
    11	    send_all_outbound, spawn_missing_relays,
    12	};
    13	use tick::{emit_now, flush_due, next_actor_msg};
    14	
    15	use crate::kernel::Kernel;
    16	use crate::relay::{OutboundMessage, RelayRole, DEFAULT_EMIT_HZ, DEFAULT_VISIBLE_LIMIT};
    17	use crate::relay_worker::{RelayCommand, RelayEvent};
    18	use std::collections::{HashMap, HashSet};
    19	use std::sync::mpsc::{self, Receiver, Sender};
    20	use std::time::{Duration, Instant};
    21	
    22	/// Actor command variants.  The `actor` module is private (`mod actor`, not
    23	/// `pub mod actor`), so this `pub` is only reachable from outside the crate
    24	/// through the `testing` re-export gate.  In normal (non-test-support) builds
    25	/// nothing re-exports these items, so they remain effectively crate-private.
    26	#[derive(Debug)]
    27	pub enum ActorCommand {
    28	    Start { visible_limit: usize, emit_hz: u32 },
    29	    Configure { visible_limit: usize, emit_hz: u32 },
    30	    OpenAuthor { pubkey: String },
    31	    OpenThread { event_id: String },
    32	    OpenFirehoseTag { tag: String },
    33	    ClaimProfile { pubkey: String, consumer_id: String },
    34	    ReleaseProfile { pubkey: String, consumer_id: String },
    35	    CloseAuthor { pubkey: String },
    36	    CloseThread { event_id: String },
    37	    Stop,
    38	    Reset,
    39	    Shutdown,
    40	}
    41	
    42	pub(super) enum ActorMsg {
    43	    Command(ActorCommand),
    44	    Relay(RelayEvent),
    45	}
    46	
    47	pub(super) struct RelayControl {
    48	    pub(super) generation: u64,
    49	    pub(super) tx: Sender<RelayCommand>,
    50	}
    51	
    52	pub fn run_actor(command_rx: Receiver<ActorCommand>, update_tx: Sender<String>) {
    53	    let (actor_tx, actor_rx) = mpsc::channel();
    54	    bridge_commands(command_rx, actor_tx.clone());
    55	    let (relay_tx, relay_rx) = mpsc::channel();
    56	    bridge_relays(relay_rx, actor_tx.clone());
    57	
    58	    let mut kernel = Kernel::new(DEFAULT_VISIBLE_LIMIT);
    59	    let mut relay_controls: HashMap<RelayRole, RelayControl> = HashMap::new();
    60	    let mut connected_relays = HashSet::new();
    61	    let mut next_relay_generation = 1;
    62	    let mut running = false;
    63	    let mut emit_hz = DEFAULT_EMIT_HZ;
    64	    let mut last_emit = Instant::now() - Duration::from_secs(1);
    65	    let mut startup_sent = false;
    66	
    67	    loop {
    68	        let message = match next_actor_msg(&actor_rx, &kernel, running, last_emit, emit_hz) {
    69	            Ok(Some(message)) => message,
    70	            Ok(None) => {
    71	                // Flush any time-gated view requests (e.g. contacts_deadline).
    72	                let pending = kernel.pending_view_requests();
    73	                if !pending.is_empty() {
    74	                    send_all_outbound(&relay_controls, &mut kernel, pending);
    75	                }
    76	                // Only emit when state actually changed; do not emit on every
    77	                // idle tick (D8: zero false-wakeup allocations after warmup).
    78	                if kernel.changed_since_emit() {
    79	                    emit_now(&mut kernel, running, &update_tx, &mut last_emit);
    80	                }
    81	                continue;
    82	            }
    83	            Err(()) => {
    84	                close_relays(&mut relay_controls, &mut connected_relays, &mut kernel);
    85	                return;
    86	            }
    87	        };
    88	
    89	        match message {
    90	            ActorMsg::Command(command) => {
    91	                let relays_ready = all_relays_connected(&connected_relays);
    92	                let outbound = dispatch_command(
    93	                    command,
    94	                    &mut kernel,
    95	                    &mut relay_controls,
    96	                    &relay_tx,
    97	                    &mut connected_relays,
    98	                    &update_tx,
    99	                    &mut last_emit,
   100	                    &mut next_relay_generation,
   101	                    &mut running,
   102	                    &mut emit_hz,
   103	                    &mut startup_sent,
   104	                    relays_ready,
   105	                );
   106	                let Some(outbound) = outbound else {
   107	                    return; // Shutdown
   108	                };
   109	                if running {
   110	                    send_all_outbound(&relay_controls, &mut kernel, outbound);
   111	                    if maybe_send_startup(
   112	                        running,
   113	                        &mut startup_sent,
   114	                        &connected_relays,
   115	                        &relay_controls,
   116	                        &mut kernel,
   117	                    ) {
   118	                        emit_now(&mut kernel, running, &update_tx, &mut last_emit);
   119	                    }
   120	                }
   121	            }
   122	            ActorMsg::Relay(event) => {
   123	                let role = event.role();
   124	                let generation = event.generation();
   125	                if relay_controls
   126	                    .get(&role)
   127	                    .is_none_or(|control| control.generation != generation)
   128	                {
   129	                    continue;
   130	                }
   131	                handle_relay_event(
   132	                    event,
   133	                    &mut kernel,
   134	                    &mut relay_controls,
   135	                    &mut connected_relays,
   136	                    &update_tx,
   137	                    &mut last_emit,
   138	                    &mut startup_sent,
   139	                    running,
   140	                );
   141	            }
   142	        }
   143	
   144	        if flush_due(&kernel, running, last_emit, emit_hz) {
   145	            emit_now(&mut kernel, running, &update_tx, &mut last_emit);
   146	        }
   147	    }
   148	}
   149	
   150	#[allow(clippy::too_many_arguments)]
   151	fn dispatch_command(
   152	    command: ActorCommand,
   153	    kernel: &mut Kernel,
   154	    relay_controls: &mut HashMap<RelayRole, RelayControl>,
   155	    relay_tx: &Sender<RelayEvent>,
   156	    connected_relays: &mut HashSet<RelayRole>,
   157	    update_tx: &Sender<String>,
   158	    last_emit: &mut Instant,
   159	    next_relay_generation: &mut u64,
   160	    running: &mut bool,
   161	    emit_hz: &mut u32,
   162	    startup_sent: &mut bool,
   163	    relays_ready: bool,
   164	) -> Option<Vec<OutboundMessage>> {
   165	    match command {
   166	        ActorCommand::Start {
   167	            visible_limit,
   168	            emit_hz: hz,
   169	        } => {
   170	            *running = true;
   171	            *emit_hz = hz;
   172	            *startup_sent = false;
   173	            kernel.set_visible_limit(visible_limit);
   174	            kernel.start();
   175	            spawn_missing_relays(relay_controls, relay_tx, kernel, next_relay_generation);
   176	            emit_now(kernel, *running, update_tx, last_emit);
   177	            Some(Vec::new())
   178	        }
   179	        ActorCommand::Configure {
   180	            visible_limit,
   181	            emit_hz: hz,
   182	        } => {
   183	            *emit_hz = hz;
   184	            kernel.set_visible_limit(visible_limit);
   185	            emit_now(kernel, *running, update_tx, last_emit);
   186	            Some(Vec::new())
   187	        }
   188	        ActorCommand::OpenAuthor { pubkey } => {
   189	            let outbound = kernel.open_author(pubkey, relays_ready);
   190	            emit_now(kernel, *running, update_tx, last_emit);
   191	            Some(outbound)
   192	        }
   193	        ActorCommand::OpenThread { event_id } => {
   194	            let outbound = kernel.open_thread(event_id, relays_ready);
   195	            emit_now(kernel, *running, update_tx, last_emit);
   196	            Some(outbound)
   197	        }
   198	        ActorCommand::OpenFirehoseTag { tag } => {
   199	            let outbound = kernel.open_firehose_tag(tag, relays_ready);
   200	            emit_now(kernel, *running, update_tx, last_emit);
   201	            Some(outbound)
   202	        }
   203	        ActorCommand::ClaimProfile {
   204	            pubkey,
   205	            consumer_id,
   206	        } => {
   207	            let outbound = kernel.claim_profile(pubkey, consumer_id, relays_ready);
   208	            emit_now(kernel, *running, update_tx, last_emit);
   209	            Some(outbound)
   210	        }
   211	        ActorCommand::ReleaseProfile {
   212	            pubkey,
   213	            consumer_id,
   214	        } => {
   215	            let outbound = kernel.release_profile(&pubkey, &consumer_id);
   216	            emit_now(kernel, *running, update_tx, last_emit);
   217	            Some(outbound)
   218	        }
   219	        ActorCommand::CloseAuthor { pubkey } => {
   220	            let outbound = kernel.close_author(&pubkey);
   221	            emit_now(kernel, *running, update_tx, last_emit);
   222	            Some(outbound)
   223	        }
   224	        ActorCommand::CloseThread { event_id } => {
   225	            let outbound = kernel.close_thread(&event_id);
   226	            emit_now(kernel, *running, update_tx, last_emit);
   227	            Some(outbound)
   228	        }
   229	        ActorCommand::Stop => {
   230	            *running = false;
   231	            *startup_sent = false;
   232	            close_relays(relay_controls, connected_relays, kernel);
   233	            emit_now(kernel, *running, update_tx, last_emit);
   234	            Some(Vec::new())
   235	        }
   236	        ActorCommand::Reset => {
   237	            close_relays(relay_controls, connected_relays, kernel);
   238	            *kernel = Kernel::new(kernel.visible_limit());
   239	            *startup_sent = false;
   240	            if *running {
   241	                kernel.start();
   242	                spawn_missing_relays(relay_controls, relay_tx, kernel, next_relay_generation);
   243	            }
   244	            emit_now(kernel, *running, update_tx, last_emit);
   245	            Some(Vec::new())
   246	        }
   247	        ActorCommand::Shutdown => {
   248	            close_relays(relay_controls, connected_relays, kernel);
   249	            None
   250	        }
   251	    }
   252	}
   253	
   254	#[allow(clippy::too_many_arguments)]
   255	fn handle_relay_event(
   256	    event: RelayEvent,
   257	    kernel: &mut Kernel,
   258	    relay_controls: &mut HashMap<RelayRole, RelayControl>,
   259	    connected_relays: &mut HashSet<RelayRole>,
   260	    update_tx: &Sender<String>,
   261	    last_emit: &mut Instant,
   262	    startup_sent: &mut bool,
   263	    running: bool,
   264	) {
   265	    match event {
   266	        RelayEvent::Connected { role, .. } => {
   267	            connected_relays.insert(role);
   268	            kernel.relay_connected(role);
   269	            maybe_send_startup(
   270	                running,
   271	                startup_sent,
   272	                connected_relays,
   273	                relay_controls,
   274	                kernel,
   275	            );
   276	            emit_now(kernel, running, update_tx, last_emit);
   277	        }
   278	        RelayEvent::Failed { role, error, .. } => {
   279	            connected_relays.remove(&role);
   280	            *startup_sent = false;
   281	            kernel.relay_failed(role, error);
   282	            emit_now(kernel, running, update_tx, last_emit);
   283	        }
   284	        RelayEvent::Closed { role, .. } => {
   285	            connected_relays.remove(&role);
   286	            *startup_sent = false;
   287	            kernel.relay_closed(role);
   288	            emit_now(kernel, running, update_tx, last_emit);
   289	        }
   290	        RelayEvent::Message { role, message, .. } if running => {
   291	            let mut outbound = kernel.handle_message(role, message);
   292	            outbound.extend(kernel.pending_view_requests());
   293	            send_all_outbound(relay_controls, kernel, outbound);
   294	        }
   295	        RelayEvent::Message { .. } => {}
   296	    }
   297	}

    45	        if can_send {

    52	
    53	    pub(crate) fn open_firehose_tag(
    54	        &mut self,
    55	        tag: String,
    56	        can_send: bool,
    57	    ) -> Vec<OutboundMessage> {
    58	        let tag = tag.trim().trim_start_matches('#').to_lowercase();
    59	        if tag.is_empty() {
    60	            return Vec::new();
    61	        }
    62	        match self.diagnostic_firehose.as_mut() {
    63	            Some(interest) if interest.key == tag => {
    64	                interest.refcount = interest.refcount.saturating_add(1);
    65	                return Vec::new();
    66	            }
    67	            _ => {
    68	                self.diagnostic_firehose = Some(ViewInterest {
    69	                    key: tag.clone(),
    70	                    refcount: 1,
    71	                });
    72	            }
    73	        }
    74	        self.changed_since_emit = true;
    75	        self.log(format!("open diagnostic firehose #{tag}"));
    76	
    77	        if can_send {
    78	            self.firehose_requests()
    79	        } else {
    80	            self.log("diagnostic firehose queued until relay connects");
    81	            Vec::new()
    82	        }
    83	    }
    84	
    85	    pub(crate) fn claim_profile(
    86	        &mut self,
    87	        pubkey: String,
    88	        consumer_id: String,
    89	        can_send: bool,
    90	    ) -> Vec<OutboundMessage> {

     4	    pub(crate) fn make_update(&mut self, running: bool) -> String {

    14	        if let Some(value) = last_event_to_emit_ms {

codex
Not merge-ready.

**Findings**
- [crates/nmp-testing/bin/ffi-stress/s3_snapshot_pressure.rs](/Users/pablofernandez/Work/nostr-multi-platform/crates/nmp-testing/bin/ffi-stress/s3_snapshot_pressure.rs:119), [s4](/Users/pablofernandez/Work/nostr-multi-platform/crates/nmp-testing/bin/ffi-stress/s4_reconciler_backpressure.rs:112), [s5](/Users/pablofernandez/Work/nostr-multi-platform/crates/nmp-testing/bin/ffi-stress/s5_reentrancy.rs:137): `configure` avoids relay workers, but these scenarios then do not create the required event pressure. `open_firehose_tag` only queues until relay connects when not started. Fix with mock relay/test-support trace injection so S3 gets 100k stored events and S4/S5 get real event inflow without external relay workers.

- [crates/nmp-testing/bin/ffi-stress/s1_mount_unmount.rs](/Users/pablofernandez/Work/nostr-multi-platform/crates/nmp-testing/bin/ffi-stress/s1_mount_unmount.rs:123): G-S1 is weakened: cycles gate is 60% not simulator 90%, RSS is 10 MiB not 5 MiB, final refcount/unmatched claim/leak/listener CPU gates are missing. Fix to enforce `gates.md` exactly, or label as non-canonical smoke test.

- [crates/nmp-testing/bin/ffi-stress/s2_dispatch_flood.rs](/Users/pablofernandez/Work/nostr-multi-platform/crates/nmp-testing/bin/ffi-stress/s2_dispatch_flood.rs:156): G-S2 is incomplete: dispatches are 90% of nominal, actor backlog peak is not measured, dropped send failures are not measured, main-thread hitch gate is absent. Fix by exposing/recording `actor_queue_depth` and send failure count; enforce full numeric gates.

- [crates/nmp-testing/bin/ffi-stress/s3_snapshot_pressure.rs](/Users/pablofernandez/Work/nostr-multi-platform/crates/nmp-testing/bin/ffi-stress/s3_snapshot_pressure.rs:40): S3 measures callback body time after JSON serialization already happened, so `callback_p99_ms` is not Rust serialization wall time. It also misses Swift `apply_us`, allocations-per-emit, and temporary-peak gates. Fix instrumentation around serialization/update production plus allocator snapshots per emit.

- [crates/nmp-testing/bin/ffi-stress/allocator.rs](/Users/pablofernandez/Work/nostr-multi-platform/crates/nmp-testing/bin/ffi-stress/allocator.rs:78): D8 is not honored as “zero per-event allocations after warmup”; transient per-cycle allocations explicitly pass, and only S1 gates allocator data. Fix by gating gross allocation count/bytes in the hot window where D8 requires zero, and add the G-S3 allocation gates.

- [crates/nmp-testing/bin/ffi-stress/s4_reconciler_backpressure.rs](/Users/pablofernandez/Work/nostr-multi-platform/crates/nmp-testing/bin/ffi-stress/s4_reconciler_backpressure.rs:201): S4 hardcodes `dropped_emits == 0` and uses emit-count delta as backlog. It does not check actor queue depth, stale-rev drops, or apply-after-resume burst. Fix with actual expected-vs-delivered emit accounting and iOS/XCUITest-side metrics.

- [crates/nmp-testing/bin/ffi-stress/s5_reentrancy.rs](/Users/pablofernandez/Work/nostr-multi-platform/crates/nmp-testing/bin/ffi-stress/s5_reentrancy.rs:79): S5 watchdog only runs after the callback returns; a real deadlock hangs instead of producing a gate failure. It also counts dispatch calls, not processed reentrant actions/order. Fix with an external timeout/heartbeat and verify the subsequent emit reflects the reentrant `OpenAuthor`.

- [crates/nmp-testing/bin/ffi-stress/report.rs](/Users/pablofernandez/Work/nostr-multi-platform/crates/nmp-testing/bin/ffi-stress/report.rs:54): output path is `docs/perf/m10.5/S1_MOUNT_UNMOUNT`, not CI’s `docs/perf/m10.5/S1/{report.md,metrics.json}`. Fix scenario directory naming.

- [justfile](/Users/pablofernandez/Work/nostr-multi-platform/justfile:52): `stress-gate-fast` runs S1/S2/S3/S5 only. Individual S1-S5 recipes exist, but the pre-merge fast gate omits S4.

**Fine**
- `nmp_app_configure` vs `nmp_app_start`: fix is applied across all stress scenarios; no `nmp_app_start` call in `ffi-stress`.
- File sizes are under 300 LOC; none exceed the 500 hard ceiling.
- No TODO/FIXME/unimplemented in the new `ffi-stress` files.
- Gate failure exit path is non-zero: [main.rs](/Users/pablofernandez/Work/nostr-multi-platform/crates/nmp-testing/bin/ffi-stress/main.rs:117) exits `2`.
- `cargo check -p nmp-testing --bin ffi-stress` passes.
